metadata.title: LSM-Raft: Optimizing Raft for LSM-tree Store
metadata.titleTranslation: LSM-Raft：面向 LSM-tree 存储的 Raft 优化
metadata.venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data, Volume 3, Issue 6 (SIGMOD), Article 340

sections.title: Abstract
sections.originalMarkdown: The Raft consensus algorithm ensures strong consistency by replicating a totally ordered log of operations. However, this strict ordering introduces significant redundancy in databases built on Log-Structured Merge (LSM) trees, where follower-side log transmission and compaction often duplicate storage and computation. In this paper, we present LSM-Raft, a generalized extension of Raft that models the replicated log as a sequence of elements—comprising both atomic entries and compacted SSTables. By aligning log semantics and state machine behavior with LSM-tree principles, LSM-Raft enables more compact and efficient replication while preserving correctness. We generalize Raft’s core safety properties to accommodate the relaxed log structure and formally verify them using TLA+. Moreover, we propose a cost-aware synchronization strategy that dynamically replaces delayed raw entries with semantically equivalent SSTables, minimizing transmission and compaction overhead. Experimental results show that LSM-Raft improves overall throughput and significantly reduces resource utilization under real-world workloads, demonstrating its practicality in high-ingest, compaction-intensive environments.
sections.translationMarkdown: Raft 共识算法通过复制一个全序操作日志来保证强一致性。然而，这种严格排序给基于 Log-Structured Merge（LSM）tree 构建的数据库带来了大量冗余：follower 端的日志传输与 compaction 往往会造成存储和计算的重复。本文提出 LSM-Raft，它是 Raft 的一种广义扩展，将复制日志建模为一个元素序列，其中既包含原子 entry，也包含经过 compaction 的 SSTable。通过使日志语义和状态机行为与 LSM-tree 原理保持一致，LSM-Raft 在保证正确性的同时，实现了更加紧凑、高效的复制。我们对 Raft 的核心安全性质进行了推广，使其能够适应更宽松的日志结构，并使用 TLA+ 对这些性质进行了形式化验证。此外，我们提出了一种成本感知的同步策略，可使用语义等价的 SSTable 动态替换延迟传输的原始 entry，从而最大限度地降低传输和 compaction 开销。实验结果表明，在真实工作负载下，LSM-Raft 提高了整体吞吐量，并显著降低了资源利用量，证明了它在高写入、compaction 密集型环境中的实用性。

sections.title: 1 INTRODUCTION
sections.originalMarkdown: In distributed systems, ensuring both strong consistency and high performance is a fundamental but often conflicting objective. Consensus protocols like Paxos [27, 28, 31, 33] and Raft [21, 24, 30, 35, 36, 42, 43, 45, 46] provide correctness guarantees but introduce performance overheads, particularly in systems with high write throughput. LSM-tree-based storage engines [15, 32, 34, 39] are widely adopted to mitigate such overheads by optimizing write performance through sequential and batched writes. Due to their write-friendly nature, LSM-trees have become increasingly popular across a broad range of databases [1, 3, 5]. In this paper, we explore how the Raft consensus protocol can be optimized for such LSM-based systems without compromising consistency or fault tolerance.
sections.translationMarkdown: 在分布式系统中，同时保证强一致性和高性能是一项基础但往往相互冲突的目标。Paxos [27, 28, 31, 33] 和 Raft [21, 24, 30, 35, 36, 42, 43, 45, 46] 等共识协议能够提供正确性保证，但也会引入性能开销，尤其是在写吞吐量较高的系统中。基于 LSM-tree 的存储引擎 [15, 32, 34, 39] 通过顺序写和批量写来优化写性能，因而被广泛用于缓解此类开销。由于其写友好的特性，LSM-tree 已在众多数据库中日益普及 [1, 3, 5]。本文探讨如何针对这类基于 LSM 的系统优化 Raft 共识协议，同时不损害一致性或容错能力。

sections.title: 1.1 Motivation
sections.originalMarkdown:
Raft has emerged as a widely adopted consensus protocol due to its understandability and strong correctness guarantees [35, 36, 43]. It ensures consistency by replicating a totally ordered sequence of log entries across all nodes and enforcing deterministic state transitions. Its safety properties—election safety, log matching, leader completeness, and state machine safety—form the foundation for replicated systems requiring fault tolerance [30, 38].

However, the standard Raft design exhibits inefficiencies when applied to LSM-based databases [22, 41]. These databases ingest data at high frequency, periodically flush logs to disk, and merge them into compacted SSTables for long-term storage. Under traditional Raft, each follower replicates raw logs and independently performs identical compaction steps, leading to redundant computation and I/O. Furthermore, when a follower falls behind, it must receive a large backlog of fine-grained entries, even though the leader may have already compacted those entries into a single SSTable.

Such inefficiencies have been observed in Raft-based storage engines like Apache Ratis [24], which treat the log as a flat sequence of atomic entries and miss the semantic equivalence between compacted SSTables and their constituent logs. As a result, they forgo optimization opportunities in modern storage engines that are already compaction-aware.

Moreover, the compaction overhead in LSM trees is non-negligible. Studies show that write amplification can increase significantly when compaction is redundantly executed across all replicas [13, 16, 23, 44]. While advanced compression techniques such as ELF [16, 29] alleviate some of the I/O burden, the fundamental replication semantics of Raft still prevent full reuse of compacted data.

**Figure 1.** Motivation of LSM-Raft: redundant flushing and compaction across nodes. In this three-node Raft cluster, the memory region M contains committed entries 0 through 9. The on-disk region Lᵢ reflects the LSM-based storage layout. Dashed SSTables have been compacted into solid SSTables on the Leader. While Follower 1 has begun receiving new data, Follower 2 is still synchronizing earlier logs, yet will repeat the same compaction steps as the Leader.

**Example 1.** As illustrated in Figure 1, we consider a typical Raft deployment consisting of three nodes. Data points with timestamps 0 through 9 have already been written to memory, replicated across a quorum of nodes, and thus committed. The system is now ingesting the next data point, timestamp 10. Follower 1, which maintains stable connectivity, has received all entries up to index 9, allowing commitment to proceed.

In contrast, Follower 2 lags behind due to slow log synchronization and has received only entry 3. In such scenarios, Raft continues transmitting raw logs sequentially. However, in LSM-based databases, all flushing and compaction operations performed on the leader will be redundantly executed on followers. This includes the creation of SSTables, their merging, and tiered storage reorganization. Consequently, even though the data is semantically equivalent, followers incur duplicated cost, creating performance bottlenecks that Raft was never designed to address.

This motivates our work: optimizing Raft for LSM-based systems to eliminate redundant processing while preserving strong consistency and fault tolerance. We propose a new protocol, LSM-Raft, that decouples logical log replication from physical log representation and enables followers to reuse compacted SSTables produced by the leader. This approach improves overall efficiency without sacrificing safety or correctness.
sections.translationMarkdown:
由于易于理解且具有很强的正确性保证，Raft 已成为一种得到广泛采用的共识协议 [35, 36, 43]。它在所有节点之间复制一个全序的日志 entry 序列，并强制执行确定性的状态转换，以此保证一致性。其安全性质——选举安全性、日志匹配、leader 完备性和状态机安全性——构成了要求容错能力的复制系统的基础 [30, 38]。

然而，将标准 Raft 设计应用于基于 LSM 的数据库时会出现低效问题 [22, 41]。这类数据库以很高的频率写入数据，周期性地将日志 flush 到磁盘，并将它们合并为经过 compaction 的 SSTable，以便长期存储。在传统 Raft 中，每个 follower 都会复制原始日志并独立执行相同的 compaction 步骤，从而导致重复计算和 I/O。此外，当 follower 落后时，即使 leader 可能已将相应 entry compact 成单个 SSTable，它仍必须接收大量积压的细粒度 entry。

这类低效现象已经出现在 Apache Ratis [24] 等基于 Raft 的存储引擎中。这些引擎将日志视为由原子 entry 构成的扁平序列，忽略了经过 compaction 的 SSTable 与构成它的日志之间的语义等价性。因此，它们错失了现代存储引擎中已有的、利用 compaction 感知能力进行优化的机会。

此外，LSM tree 的 compaction 开销不可忽视。研究表明，当所有副本都重复执行 compaction 时，写放大会显著增加 [13, 16, 23, 44]。尽管 ELF [16, 29] 等先进压缩技术缓解了部分 I/O 负担，但 Raft 的基本复制语义仍然阻碍了对 compact 后数据的充分复用。

**图 1.** LSM-Raft 的研究动机：跨节点的重复 flush 与 compaction。在这个三节点 Raft 集群中，内存区域 M 包含已提交的 entry 0 至 9。磁盘区域 Lᵢ 展示了基于 LSM 的存储布局。在 Leader 上，虚线 SSTable 已经被 compact 成实线 SSTable。Follower 1 已开始接收新数据，而 Follower 2 仍在同步较早的日志，但之后仍会重复 Leader 已执行的相同 compaction 步骤。

**例 1.** 如图 1 所示，我们考虑一个由三个节点组成的典型 Raft 部署。时间戳为 0 至 9 的数据点已经写入内存，并被复制到达到 quorum 的节点上，因此已经提交。系统现在正在写入下一个数据点，即时间戳 10。保持稳定连接的 Follower 1 已收到截至索引 9 的全部 entry，因此提交过程可以继续。

相比之下，Follower 2 因日志同步缓慢而落后，目前只收到 entry 3。在这种情况下，Raft 会继续按顺序传输原始日志。然而，在基于 LSM 的数据库中，leader 执行的全部 flush 和 compaction 操作都会在 follower 上重复执行，其中包括 SSTable 的创建、合并和分层存储重组。因此，即使数据在语义上等价，follower 仍会承担重复开销，从而形成 Raft 在设计时从未考虑过的性能瓶颈。

这正是本文工作的动机：针对基于 LSM 的系统优化 Raft，在保持强一致性和容错能力的同时消除重复处理。我们提出一种新协议 LSM-Raft，它将逻辑日志复制与物理日志表示解耦，使 follower 能够复用 leader 生成的、经过 compaction 的 SSTable。该方法在不牺牲安全性或正确性的前提下提高了整体效率。

sections.title: 1.2 The LSM-Raft Approach
sections.originalMarkdown:
To address the inefficiencies in Raft over LSM-based storage engines, we propose LSM-Raft, a generalized version of Raft that redefines the structure and semantics of the replicated log. In LSM-Raft, the log is no longer a simple sequence of atomic entries. Instead, it consists of generalized elements, each of which may represent either a single raw data point or a compacted SSTable generated through LSM flush and merge.

Each element is annotated with a metadata tuple containing the Raft term, global index, and compaction level. This metadata allows us to maintain global log order while supporting flexible internal structure. Elements may overlap in data range, which reflects the behavior of real LSM trees but does not violate Raft consistency as long as the resulting state is preserved.

We redefine the state machine as an LSM state function, which takes the log as input and simulates LSM compaction to compute the final database state. By modeling the state as a sequence of SSTables, we capture the merge behavior of LSM engines and enable correctness reasoning under compaction-aware semantics.

LSM-Raft also supports a flexible log transmission protocol. When followers fall behind, instead of sending individual log entries, the leader may select an SSTable that is semantically equivalent to the missing sequence and transmit it directly. To support this, we introduce the notion of log equivalence: two log sequences are equivalent if they produce the same LSM state. This allows us to replace raw logs with compressed SSTables without breaking consistency.

To make this replacement efficient, we define a cost model that evaluates the trade-off between transmission size and compaction overhead. We then design a fast replacement decision algorithm that runs in constant time by comparing metadata only. This enables real-time optimization of replication performance in high-ingestion scenarios.

Finally, the LSM-Raft design preserves Raft’s fault tolerance and consistency guarantees. We generalize log matching, leader completeness, and state machine safety to work under the LSM model, and we formally verify these properties using TLA+ [9]. Thus, LSM-Raft maintains correctness while achieving greater efficiency in LSM-based systems.

**Table 1. Frequently used notations**

| Symbol | Descriptions |
|---|---|
| M | the MemTable in LSM |
| Lᵢ | the ith level in LSM |
| Eᵢ | the Raft log element(log entry or SSTable) |
| L | the Raft log |
| S | the LSM state |
| Φ | the LSM state function |
| ρ | the compression ratio of SSTables |
| C_trans | the transmission cost |
| C_comp | the compaction cost |
| C | the total cost |
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了解决 Raft 在基于 LSM 的存储引擎上的低效问题，我们提出 LSM-Raft。它是 Raft 的一种广义版本，重新定义了复制日志的结构和语义。在 LSM-Raft 中，日志不再是简单的原子 entry 序列，而是由广义元素构成；每个元素既可以表示一个原始数据点，也可以表示通过 LSM flush 和 merge 生成的、经过 compaction 的 SSTable。

每个元素都附带一个元数据元组，其中包含 Raft term、全局索引和 compaction level。这些元数据使我们能够在支持灵活内部结构的同时维持日志的全局顺序。元素的数据范围可以相互重叠，这反映了真实 LSM tree 的行为；只要最终状态得到保持，就不会违反 Raft 的一致性。

我们将状态机重新定义为 LSM 状态函数。该函数以日志为输入，通过模拟 LSM compaction 来计算数据库的最终状态。通过将状态建模为一个 SSTable 序列，我们能够刻画 LSM 引擎的合并行为，并在 compaction 感知语义下推理正确性。

LSM-Raft 还支持灵活的日志传输协议。当 follower 落后时，leader 可以不发送单独的日志 entry，而是选择一个与缺失序列语义等价的 SSTable 并直接传输。为此，我们引入日志等价概念：如果两个日志序列产生相同的 LSM 状态，那么它们就是等价的。这样便可以用压缩后的 SSTable 替换原始日志，同时不破坏一致性。

为了高效地完成这种替换，我们定义了一个成本模型，用于评估传输大小与 compaction 开销之间的权衡。随后，我们设计了一种快速的替换决策算法，它只需比较元数据，便可在常数时间内运行。这使系统能够在高写入场景中实时优化复制性能。

最后，LSM-Raft 的设计保留了 Raft 的容错与一致性保证。我们对日志匹配、leader 完备性和状态机安全性进行了推广，使其适用于 LSM 模型，并使用 TLA+ 对这些性质进行了形式化验证 [9]。因此，LSM-Raft 在保持正确性的同时，在基于 LSM 的系统中实现了更高的效率。

**表 1. 常用符号**

| 符号 | 描述 |
|---|---|
| M | LSM 中的 MemTable |
| Lᵢ | LSM 中的第 i 层 |
| Eᵢ | Raft 日志元素（日志 entry 或 SSTable） |
| L | Raft 日志 |
| S | LSM 状态 |
| Φ | LSM 状态函数 |
| ρ | SSTable 的压缩率 |
| C_trans | 传输成本 |
| C_comp | compaction 成本 |
| C | 总成本 |

sections.title: 1.3 Contributions
sections.originalMarkdown:
While LSM and Raft are established techniques, their non-trivial integration in LSM-Raft introduces new abstractions, provable correctness under generalization, and efficiency-driven mechanisms beyond engineering. Our major contributions in this paper are as follows.

1. **Design Novelty:** We generalize Raft’s log replication to support the transmission of compacted SSTables (Algorithm 1), enabled by formal abstractions such as generalized log elements (Definition 1). This unifies the consensus and storage layers, allowing Raft to replicate both logical operations and physical storage states.
2. **Theoretical Formulation:** To preserve safety under the new design, we generalize Raft’s core properties—Log Matching, Leader Completeness, and State Machine Safety (Properties 1, 2, 3)—and formally verify them using TLA+ (Section 4.3). It provides a strong theoretical foundation for safely deploying LSM-Raft in systems.
3. **Technical Contribution:** We propose a cost-aware synchronization strategy (Algorithm 2) that dynamically selects between raw entries and compacted SSTables based on transmission and compaction cost. This introduces cost sensitivity into consensus logic, enabling real-time, storage-aware decisions at the leader.
4. **Practical Generality:** We implement LSM-Raft in both TiDB [7, 22] and Apache IoTDB [1, 41], and evaluate it with real-world workloads, which demonstrate the practical generality of LSM-Raft. Our results show improvements in overall throughput and significant reductions in CPU, compaction and network cost with unaffected fault tolerance.
sections.translationMarkdown:
尽管 LSM 和 Raft 都是成熟技术，但二者在 LSM-Raft 中的非平凡集成引入了新的抽象、推广后的可证明正确性，以及超越单纯工程实现的效率驱动机制。本文的主要贡献如下。

1. **设计创新：** 我们对 Raft 的日志复制进行了推广，使其支持传输经过 compaction 的 SSTable（算法 1）；这种能力建立在广义日志元素（定义 1）等形式化抽象之上。该设计统一了共识层与存储层，使 Raft 既能复制逻辑操作，也能复制物理存储状态。
2. **理论形式化：** 为了在新设计下保持安全性，我们对 Raft 的核心性质——日志匹配、leader 完备性和状态机安全性（性质 1、2、3）——进行了推广，并使用 TLA+ 对其进行形式化验证（第 4.3 节）。这为在系统中安全部署 LSM-Raft 提供了坚实的理论基础。
3. **技术贡献：** 我们提出一种成本感知的同步策略（算法 2），根据传输成本和 compaction 成本，在原始 entry 与经过 compaction 的 SSTable 之间动态选择。该方法在共识逻辑中引入成本敏感性，使 leader 能够实时做出存储感知的决策。
4. **实践通用性：** 我们在 TiDB [7, 22] 和 Apache IoTDB [1, 41] 中实现了 LSM-Raft，并使用真实工作负载对其进行了评估，证明了 LSM-Raft 的实践通用性。结果表明，在不影响容错能力的情况下，LSM-Raft 提高了整体吞吐量，并显著降低了 CPU、compaction 和网络成本。

sections.title: 2 PRELIMINARIES
sections.originalMarkdown: To facilitate understanding of the LSM-Raft design and its applicability, we present three core areas of background knowledge: the LSM-tree storage engine, the Raft consensus protocol, and the prevailing database systems.
sections.translationMarkdown: 为了便于理解 LSM-Raft 的设计及其适用性，我们介绍三个核心背景领域：LSM-tree 存储引擎、Raft 共识协议以及主流数据库系统。

sections.title: 2.1 LSM-Tree Storage Engine
sections.originalMarkdown:
The Log-Structured Merge (LSM) tree [32, 34] is widely adopted in high-ingestion databases [3–5, 41]. Its popularity stems from its write-optimized architecture, which converts random updates into sequential, batched disk operations. This design minimizes write amplification and enables high-throughput ingestion, making it suitable for workloads with sustained write pressure.

The LSM architecture buffers writes in an in-memory MemTable, which is asynchronously flushed to disk as immutable sorted files called SSTables. These files are managed in a tiered layout across multiple levels (L0, L1, L2, . . . ). New SSTables are first written to Level 0 (L0), where files may overlap in key range. Subsequent levels contain non-overlapping SSTables, each covering a disjoint key range to support efficient lookups and merges.

To maintain system performance, LSM trees use compaction to merge SSTables across levels, reduce redundancy, reclaim storage, and preserve key ordering. For example, when L0 accumulates too many overlapping files, they are compacted into L1 to reduce read amplification. This multi-level compaction process balances write efficiency, read latency, and disk space usage, forming the foundation of LSM-tree behavior. Understanding this hierarchy is crucial when designing log replication protocols that must reflect consistent and deterministic storage states across replicas.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Log-Structured Merge（LSM）tree [32, 34] 被广泛应用于高写入数据库 [3–5, 41]。它之所以流行，是因为其面向写优化的架构能够将随机更新转换为顺序、批量的磁盘操作。这种设计可以最大限度地降低写放大并实现高吞吐量写入，因此适用于持续承受写压力的工作负载。

LSM 架构先将写入缓存在内存中的 MemTable 中，随后将其异步 flush 到磁盘，形成称为 SSTable 的不可变有序文件。这些文件以分层方式组织在多个 level（L0、L1、L2……）中。新的 SSTable 首先写入 Level 0（L0），其中各文件的键范围可以重叠。后续 level 包含互不重叠的 SSTable，每个 SSTable 覆盖一个不相交的键范围，以支持高效查询和合并。

为了维持系统性能，LSM tree 使用 compaction 跨 level 合并 SSTable、减少冗余、回收存储空间并保持键有序。例如，当 L0 中积累了过多键范围重叠的文件时，系统会将其 compact 到 L1，以减少读放大。这种多 level compaction 过程在写效率、读延迟和磁盘空间使用之间取得平衡，构成了 LSM-tree 行为的基础。在设计必须使不同副本体现出一致且确定的存储状态的日志复制协议时，理解这种层次结构至关重要。

sections.title: 2.2 Raft Consensus Protocol
sections.originalMarkdown:
In contrast to Byzantine fault-tolerant (BFT) protocols [11, 12, 18], Raft [21, 35, 36, 45] follows a crash fault-tolerant (CFT) model and ensure strong consistency in distributed systems. It includes log replication, leader election, and majority-based commit semantics. As long as a majority of nodes are functioning, the protocol guarantees safe and consistent log advancement.

In Raft, each log entry is uniquely identified by a pair: term (the election round in which the leader was elected) and index (its position in the log). During leader election, nodes vote for a candidate whose log is at least as up-to-date as theirs, based on the latest term and index. This ensures that only the most recent and consistent log is replicated. The elected leader then becomes the sole coordinator of client requests and log replication until the next election.

Raft performs log replication as follows: when the leader receives a client request, it appends the operation as a new log entry in its local log and concurrently sends AppendEntries RPCs to all followers. Followers either accept and append the entry to their logs or reject if a mismatch is detected. The leader retries until a majority of followers acknowledge the entry. Once replicated to a quorum, the leader commits the entry and applies it to the state machine. This commit step ensures that only entries replicated to a majority are considered durable and visible to reads.

As a variant of Raft [36], LSM-Raft adopts the standard leader-based read model, in which all client reads are served by the leader node. As stated in the extended version [35], Section 6, "Client requests in Raft are processed through the leader." Before responding to a read request, the leader ensures that the data has been committed by a majority of replicas through the Raft consensus process. This read model guarantees data consistency, because clients always observe the latest committed state that reflects a total order of writes.
sections.translationMarkdown:
与拜占庭容错（BFT）协议 [11, 12, 18] 不同，Raft [21, 35, 36, 45] 采用崩溃容错（CFT）模型，并保证分布式系统中的强一致性。它包括日志复制、leader 选举和基于多数派的提交语义。只要多数节点正常工作，该协议就能保证日志安全且一致地向前推进。

在 Raft 中，每个日志 entry 都由一个二元组唯一标识：term（leader 当选时所在的选举轮次）和 index（entry 在日志中的位置）。在 leader 选举期间，节点会根据最新 term 和 index，将票投给日志至少与自身一样新的 candidate。这样可以确保仅复制最新且一致的日志。此后，直到下一次选举，当选的 leader 都是客户端请求和日志复制的唯一协调者。

Raft 按如下方式执行日志复制：当 leader 收到客户端请求时，它将该操作作为一个新的日志 entry 追加到本地日志中，并同时向所有 follower 发送 AppendEntries RPC。follower 要么接受并将该 entry 追加到自身日志，要么在检测到不匹配时拒绝。leader 会不断重试，直至多数 follower 确认该 entry。一旦 entry 被复制到达到 quorum 的节点上，leader 便会提交该 entry 并将其应用到状态机。该提交步骤确保只有被复制到多数节点的 entry 才会被认为是持久的，并对读操作可见。

作为 Raft [36] 的一个变体，LSM-Raft 采用标准的基于 leader 的读取模型，即所有客户端读取均由 leader 节点提供服务。正如扩展版本 [35] 第 6 节所述：“Raft 中的客户端请求通过 leader 处理。”在响应读取请求之前，leader 会通过 Raft 共识过程确保数据已被多数副本提交。该读取模型保证了数据一致性，因为客户端始终能观察到反映写入全序关系的最新已提交状态。

sections.title: 2.3 System Model and Target Applications
sections.originalMarkdown:
With the increasing demands for large-scale data ingestion and reliable consistency, modern databases are progressively adopting LSM-tree architectures for storage and Raft as the core distributed consensus mechanism. As discussed in previous sections, this combination has become a mainstream design for ensuring both high write performance and fault-tolerant replication.

Several production-grade systems adopt this architecture pattern. For example, TiDB [7, 22] and Apache IoTDB [1, 41] integrate LSM-tree storage engines with Raft-based replication layers. These systems span diverse application domains, including time-series workloads and transactional or hybrid OLTP/OLAP scenarios, where consistency and write-intensive performance are critical.

LSM-Raft is particularly beneficial in scenarios with continuous, high-volume data ingestion. Representative applications include industrial sensor monitoring, telemetry pipelines, and edge computing deployments- environments that demand both high throughput and strongly consistent replication guarantees.
sections.translationMarkdown:
随着大规模数据写入和可靠一致性需求不断增长，现代数据库逐渐采用 LSM-tree 架构进行存储，并将 Raft 用作核心分布式共识机制。如前文所述，这种组合已经成为兼顾高写性能和容错复制的主流设计。

若干生产级系统采用了这种架构模式。例如，TiDB [7, 22] 和 Apache IoTDB [1, 41] 将 LSM-tree 存储引擎与基于 Raft 的复制层集成。这些系统覆盖多种应用领域，包括时序工作负载、事务型场景以及混合 OLTP/OLAP 场景；在这些场景中，一致性和写密集型性能都至关重要。

LSM-Raft 尤其适用于持续进行大规模数据写入的场景。代表性应用包括工业传感器监控、遥测流水线和边缘计算部署，这些环境既要求高吞吐量，也要求强一致的复制保证。

sections.title: 3 LSM-RAFT DESIGN
sections.originalMarkdown: This section presents the architecture and core design mechanisms of LSM-Raft. Compared to traditional Raft, LSM-Raft extends the definition of logs to support level-based SSTable replication. After log generalization, we mainly focus on log replication, leader election and safety.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节介绍 LSM-Raft 的架构与核心设计机制。与传统 Raft 相比，LSM-Raft 扩展了日志的定义，以支持基于 level 的 SSTable 复制。在对日志进行推广后，我们主要关注日志复制、leader 选举和安全性。

sections.title: 3.1 Log Generalization
sections.originalMarkdown:
In traditional Raft [36], the replicated log is a totally ordered sequence of immutable entries, each tagged with a term and a globally unique, increasing index. Each entry represents a discrete command to be applied to the replicated state machine. LSM-Raft extends this notion by allowing each element in the log to represent either a single entry or a compacted SSTable.

**Definition 1 (Element).** Let 𝒟 be the domain of time-series records. An element Eᵢ is defined as either: (1) a singleton entry: Eᵢ = {e}, where e ∈ 𝒟 is a log entry; (2) a compacted SSTable: Eᵢ = {e₁, ..., eₙ} where eⱼ.timestamp < eⱼ₊₁.timestamp.

(index, term, logSpan) is annotated with each element as a metadata. Level denotes the storage level in LSM-Tree. LogSpan is newly added to denotes the indices range that the entry or SSTable covers.

$$\operatorname{LogSpan}(E) = \{e.index \quad \forall e \in E\}$$

We clarify that within the same level, each log element uses the same range size, but the index spans are non-overlapping. This design follows the standard structure of level-based SSTables in LSM-trees, where files at the same level cover disjoint key (or index) ranges to support efficient query and compaction operations [32, 34].

**Definition 2 (Log).** The replicated log in LSM-Raft is a sequence of elements:

$$\mathbf{L} = \langle E_1, E_2, \ldots, E_n \rangle$$

where each Eᵢ has a globally unique index, and elements may semantically overlap in their internal coverage (logSpan). These overlaps do not violate index ordering and are resolved by compaction-aware consistency logic.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在传统 Raft [36] 中，复制日志是由不可变 entry 构成的全序序列，每个 entry 都带有一个 term 和一个全局唯一且递增的 index。每个 entry 表示一条要应用到复制状态机的离散命令。LSM-Raft 对这一概念进行了扩展，使日志中的每个元素既可以表示一个 entry，也可以表示一个经过 compaction 的 SSTable。

**定义 1（元素）。** 令 𝒟 为时序记录的定义域。元素 Eᵢ 定义为以下两种形式之一：（1）单元素 entry：Eᵢ = {e}，其中 e ∈ 𝒟 是一个日志 entry；（2）经过 compaction 的 SSTable：Eᵢ = {e₁, ..., eₙ}，其中 eⱼ.timestamp < eⱼ₊₁.timestamp。

每个元素都附带 (index, term, logSpan) 作为元数据。Level 表示 LSM-Tree 中的存储层级。LogSpan 是新增字段，用于表示该 entry 或 SSTable 覆盖的索引范围。

$$\operatorname{LogSpan}(E) = \{e.index \quad \forall e \in E\}$$

需要说明的是，在同一 level 内，每个日志元素使用相同的范围大小，但其 index span 互不重叠。该设计遵循 LSM-tree 中基于 level 的 SSTable 标准结构：同一 level 上的文件覆盖互不相交的键（或索引）范围，以支持高效查询和 compaction 操作 [32, 34]。

**定义 2（日志）。** LSM-Raft 中的复制日志是一个元素序列：

$$\mathbf{L} = \langle E_1, E_2, \ldots, E_n \rangle$$

其中每个 Eᵢ 都具有全局唯一的 index，而各元素的内部覆盖范围（logSpan）在语义上可能重叠。这些重叠不会违反索引顺序，并由 compaction 感知的一致性逻辑处理。

sections.title: 3.2 Log Replication
sections.originalMarkdown:
In LSM-Raft, log replication must tolerate behind followers and then try to sync entries into the folllowers. This is achieved by comparing not only the index and term, but also the log span – the range of logical indices a given element covers. A consistency check involves comparing the leader’s and follower’s terms, log indices, and spans. If the terms match, the follower determines whether the incoming element aligns, overlaps, or conflicts with its own log.

**Example 2.** As shown in Figure 2, LSM-Raft reduces both transmission and compaction overhead by enabling the leader to replicate compacted SSTables directly. Importantly, this optimization does not bypass entry-level synchronization or compromise consistency; instead, it selects lower-cost synchronization paths when the system faces I/O or network bottlenecks.

**Figure 2.** The leader replicates a mix of atomic entries and compacted SSTables, and selects the transmission granularity based on each follower’s state. Follower 1 receives 6 entries (cost = 6) and 2 SSTables (cost = 2 × 0.5 × 2 = 2), totaling a transmission cost of 8, along with a compaction cost of 22 due to redundant local merging. In contrast, since Follower 2 replicates the log too slow, the leader proactively chooses to send 2 SSTables from levels L2 and L0, resulting in a lower transmission cost of 5 and avoiding unnecessary compaction.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在 LSM-Raft 中，日志复制必须能够容忍落后的 follower，并尝试将 entry 同步到这些 follower。这通过不仅比较 index 和 term，还比较 log span（即给定元素所覆盖的逻辑索引范围）来实现。一致性检查需要比较 leader 与 follower 的 term、日志 index 和 span。如果 term 匹配，follower 就判断传入元素与自身日志是对齐、重叠还是冲突。

**例 2.** 如图 2 所示，LSM-Raft 允许 leader 直接复制经过 compaction 的 SSTable，从而同时减少传输开销和 compaction 开销。重要的是，这项优化不会绕过 entry 级同步，也不会损害一致性；相反，当系统面临 I/O 或网络瓶颈时，它会选择成本更低的同步路径。

**图 2.** leader 复制由原子 entry 和经过 compaction 的 SSTable 混合组成的数据，并根据每个 follower 的状态选择传输粒度。Follower 1 接收 6 个 entry（成本 = 6）和 2 个 SSTable（成本 = 2 × 0.5 × 2 = 2），总传输成本为 8；同时，由于重复执行本地合并，其 compaction 成本为 22。相比之下，由于 Follower 2 复制日志的速度过慢，leader 主动选择发送来自 L2 和 L0 的 2 个 SSTable，从而将传输成本降至 5，并避免不必要的 compaction。

sections.title: 3.2.1 Consistency Check Algorithm
sections.originalMarkdown:
We present Algorithm 1, which describes the consistency check logic for appending a new element from the leader to the follower log. The algorithm generalizes the Raft-style preLogIndex and preLogTerm comparison by also considering the log span — the complete logical index coverage of a potentially compacted element. It abstracts the notion through the use of preLogSpan and followerLogSpan, representing the logical coverage of the leader’s and follower’s elements, respectively.

**Algorithm 1 ConsistencyCheck**

**Require:** `preLogTerm, preLogIndex, preLogSpan`  
**Require:** `followerTerm, followerLogIndex, followerLogSpan`  
**Ensure:** `ACCEPT`, `REJECT`, or `NEXTINDEX`

```text
 1: if preLogTerm == followerTerm then
 2:     if preLogIndex == followerLogIndex then
 3:         return ACCEPT                       ▷ Exact match on index
 4:     end if
 5:     if NoOverlap(preLogSpan, followerLogSpan) then
 6:         if max(preLogSpan) < min(followerLogSpan) then
 7:             delete follower log at min(followerLogSpan)
 8:             return ConsistencyCheck         ▷ Delete and Retry
 9:         else
10:             return NEXTINDEX                 ▷ Follower index behind
11:         end if
12:     end if
13:     if max(preLogSpan) == max(followerLogSpan) then
14:         return ACCEPT                        ▷ Exact match on SSTable
15:     end if
16:     if max(preLogSpan) < min(followerLogSpan) then
17:         delete follower log at min(followerLogSpan)
18:         return ConsistencyCheck              ▷ Delete and Retry
19:     end if
20:     if min(preLogSpan) > max(followerLogSpan) then
21:         return REJECT                        ▷ Follower index behind
22:     end if
23: else if preLogTerm > followerTerm then
24:     return NEXTINDEX                         ▷ Follower term behind
25: else
26:     return REJECT                            ▷ Leader is stale
27: end if
```

If spans are aligned at the boundaries, replication proceeds straightforwardly, as shown in Line 3. However, if any replica fails, span corruption and metadata inconsistency may occur—which is a common situation in Raft. As shown in Line 6 and Line 16, when the follower’s span is totally or partially ahead of the leader’s, Algorithm 1 triggers local deletion in Line 8. Conversely, as shown in Line 9 and Line 20, when the leader’s entry is ahead of the follower’s, the algorithm updates the NEXTINDEX in Line 10, and retries until an exact match is found in Lines 13 and 14. This design ensures that follower logs are correctly updated either by element deletion or by backward traversal of the leader’s log, avoiding over-acceptance that could compromise correctness.

As the standard Raft, the leader delivers data to followers synchronously. That is, our LSM-Raft have the same acknowledgment feedback information from followers for each data replication as the vanilla Raft. For example, in Algorithm 1, each follower returns acknowledgment information such as ACCEPT or REJECT, which is essential for the leader to determine log alignment, detect inconsistencies, and decide whether to send raw log entries or compacted SSTables. This interaction mirrors the original Raft behavior as described in Section 5.3 of the Raft paper [36].

Overall, this mechanism maintains Raft’s safety guarantees — including the Log Matching Property and Leader Completeness, which will be generalized later in Section 4. This abstraction forms the foundation for efficient, consistent replication in LSM-based distributed databases.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们给出算法 1，它描述了将 leader 发来的新元素追加到 follower 日志时所采用的一致性检查逻辑。该算法在 Raft 风格的 preLogIndex 和 preLogTerm 比较基础上进行了推广，还会考虑 log span，即一个可能经过 compaction 的元素所完整覆盖的逻辑索引范围。算法使用 preLogSpan 和 followerLogSpan 对这一概念进行抽象，二者分别表示 leader 元素和 follower 元素的逻辑覆盖范围。

**算法 1 ConsistencyCheck**

**输入要求：** `preLogTerm, preLogIndex, preLogSpan`  
**输入要求：** `followerTerm, followerLogIndex, followerLogSpan`  
**输出保证：** `ACCEPT`、`REJECT` 或 `NEXTINDEX`

```text
 1: if preLogTerm == followerTerm then
 2:     if preLogIndex == followerLogIndex then
 3:         return ACCEPT                       ▷ index 完全匹配
 4:     end if
 5:     if NoOverlap(preLogSpan, followerLogSpan) then
 6:         if max(preLogSpan) < min(followerLogSpan) then
 7:             删除 min(followerLogSpan) 处的 follower 日志
 8:             return ConsistencyCheck         ▷ 删除并重试
 9:         else
10:             return NEXTINDEX                 ▷ follower index 落后
11:         end if
12:     end if
13:     if max(preLogSpan) == max(followerLogSpan) then
14:         return ACCEPT                        ▷ SSTable 完全匹配
15:     end if
16:     if max(preLogSpan) < min(followerLogSpan) then
17:         删除 min(followerLogSpan) 处的 follower 日志
18:         return ConsistencyCheck              ▷ 删除并重试
19:     end if
20:     if min(preLogSpan) > max(followerLogSpan) then
21:         return REJECT                        ▷ follower index 落后
22:     end if
23: else if preLogTerm > followerTerm then
24:     return NEXTINDEX                         ▷ follower term 落后
25: else
26:     return REJECT                            ▷ leader 已过时
27: end if
```

如果两个 span 的边界对齐，复制就可以直接进行，如第 3 行所示。然而，如果任一副本发生故障，就可能出现 span 损坏和元数据不一致；这在 Raft 中很常见。如第 6 行和第 16 行所示，当 follower 的 span 完全或部分领先于 leader 的 span 时，算法 1 会在第 8 行触发本地删除。反之，如第 9 行和第 20 行所示，当 leader 的 entry 领先于 follower 时，算法会在第 10 行更新 NEXTINDEX，并不断重试，直到在第 13 和第 14 行找到完全匹配。该设计通过删除元素或在 leader 日志中向后遍历，确保 follower 日志得到正确更新，避免因过度接受而损害正确性。

与标准 Raft 一样，leader 向 follower 同步传送数据。也就是说，对于每次数据复制，LSM-Raft 从 follower 获得的确认反馈信息与原始 Raft 相同。例如，在算法 1 中，每个 follower 都会返回 ACCEPT 或 REJECT 等确认信息；这些信息对于 leader 判断日志是否对齐、检测不一致，以及决定发送原始日志 entry 还是经过 compaction 的 SSTable 至关重要。该交互与 Raft 论文 [36] 第 5.3 节所描述的原始 Raft 行为一致。

总体而言，该机制维持了 Raft 的安全保证，包括日志匹配性质和 leader 完备性；这些性质将在第 4 节进一步推广。该抽象为基于 LSM 的分布式数据库中的高效、一致复制奠定了基础。

sections.title: 3.2.2 Index Management
sections.originalMarkdown:
In LSM-Raft, the leader maintains a pair of indices for each follower: nextIndex and matchIndex. These are updated upon every successful AppendEntries RPC.

**nextIndex.** This index indicates the log index the leader will send next to the follower. It serves as the current replication position. Initially, it is set to the leader’s last log index plus one. When entries are successfully appended to a follower, it is updated as:

$$\operatorname{nextIndex} \leftarrow \max(E.\operatorname{logSpan}) + 1$$

If an inconsistency is detected, nextIndex is decremented to retry synchronization with an earlier log element.

**matchIndex.** This index reflects the highest log index the leader knows to be successfully replicated on the follower. Upon successful append, it is updated as:

$$\operatorname{matchIndex} \leftarrow \max(E.\operatorname{logSpan})$$

This field is crucial for determining when an entry is safely replicated on a quorum.

**commitIndex.** The commitIndex represents the highest log index known to be committed across a majority of nodes. In LSM-Raft, a log element is considered committed if all of its covered indices (as described by logSpan) are included in the matchIndex of a quorum of followers. This ensures that even in the presence of compaction and log rewriting, every committed entry remains durably and consistently replicated.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在 LSM-Raft 中，leader 为每个 follower 维护一对索引：nextIndex 和 matchIndex。每次 AppendEntries RPC 成功后，都会更新这两个索引。

**nextIndex。** 该索引表示 leader 接下来要向 follower 发送的日志 index，即当前复制位置。初始时，它被设置为 leader 最后一个日志 index 加一。当 entry 成功追加到 follower 后，其更新方式如下：

$$\operatorname{nextIndex} \leftarrow \max(E.\operatorname{logSpan}) + 1$$

如果检测到不一致，则递减 nextIndex，以使用更早的日志元素重新尝试同步。

**matchIndex。** 该索引表示 leader 已知在 follower 上成功复制的最高日志 index。追加成功后，其更新方式如下：

$$\operatorname{matchIndex} \leftarrow \max(E.\operatorname{logSpan})$$

该字段对于判断某个 entry 何时已被安全复制到达到 quorum 的节点上至关重要。

**commitIndex。** commitIndex 表示已知在多数节点上提交的最高日志 index。在 LSM-Raft 中，如果一个日志元素覆盖的所有 index（由 logSpan 描述）都包含在达到 quorum 的 follower 的 matchIndex 范围内，则认为该日志元素已经提交。这确保了即使存在 compaction 和日志重写，每个已提交 entry 仍然得到持久且一致的复制。

sections.title: 3.3 Safety
sections.originalMarkdown:
LSM-Raft retains Raft’s original safety guarantees, generalized for its richer log model: A candidate can only be elected if its last log covers all committed log indices known to a quorum. Only entries from the leader’s current term are committed directly; earlier ones are committed transitively. If two logs share the same index and term, their application up to that point must produce the same LSM state. These extensions are formally proven in Section 4.

**Example 3.** As shown in Figure 3, we illustrate the log replication protocol of LSM-Raft, covering leader commit advancement, follower synchronization, leader election, and crash recovery.

**(a) Commit Advancement via Shared LogSpan.** At this stage, Replica #4 has just completed synchronization of the compressed SSTable L₁⁰, which covers the log span {4, 5}. Since this SSTable has been replicated to a quorum (e.g., replicas #2 and #4), we can safely commit entries at index 4 and 5, and advance the leader’s commitIndex from 2 to 5.

If the leader crashes and a new election begins, the LSM-Raft election mechanism remains consistent with Raft: a candidate must contain all committed entries. Replica #2, holding atomic entries up to index 5, qualifies. Replica #4 also qualifies, as its L₁⁰ semantically covers {4, 5}.

**(b) #4 Becomes Leader: Synchronization by LogSpan overlap.** Now suppose replica #4 becomes the new leader. Its last log index is 6, and it initiates replication using preLogSpan = {4, 5} from L₁⁰.

- Replica #3 holds only entry #4, i.e., followerLogSpan = {4}, which is a strict subset of the leader’s span. According to Algorithm Line 16, the entry is considered outdated and will be deleted. The leader re-sends L₁⁰.
- Replica #5 holds only index 1, i.e., followerLogSpan = {1}, with no overlap with {4, 5}. According to Algorithm Line 9, it rejects the replication attempt. The leader decrements nextIndex to 3 and updates preLogSpan = {1, 2}. Since there is now an overlap ({1}), the follower deletes its entry at index 1, and then proceeds to synchronize entries from index 3 onward.

**(c) #2 Becomes Leader: Fine-Grained Entry Synchronization.** If replica #2 ecomes the leader, its preLogSpan = {5}.

- Replica #4 holds L₁⁰ with followerLogSpan = {4, 5}. The overlap includes index 5, and it matches the maximum covered index of #2, so according to Algorithm Line 13, the leader considers it up-to-date.
- Replicas #3 and #5 are handled as in (b) using followerLogSpan comparisons, resulting in synchronization of entries with higher index.

**(d) #1 Recovers: Efficient Resynchronization via SSTable.** After a stable period, the previously crashed replica #1 restarts and re-joins the cluster. A re-election confirms #2 as the leader. Since #1 is outdated, the leader transmits the compacted SSTable L₂⁰ according to Algorithm Line 23, which semantically covers all missing log entries. This enables replica #1 to synchronize to the latest state in a single transmission, avoiding redundant entry replay and reducing recovery latency and network cost.

**Figure 3.** Example for Log Replication in LSM-Raft

LSM-Raft extends Raft’s log matching principle by reasoning over LogSpan instead of exact index and term. By comparing leader and follower spans, not only redundant entries can be safely deleted and followers can synchronize via compacted SSTables, but also crash recovery can be accelerated through semantic state transfer. This mechanism significantly reduces replication overhead and improves convergence efficiency in high-ingestion scenarios.
sections.translationMarkdown:
LSM-Raft 保留了 Raft 原有的安全保证，并针对更丰富的日志模型进行了推广：只有当 candidate 的最后一段日志覆盖 quorum 已知的全部已提交日志 index 时，它才能当选。只有 leader 当前 term 中的 entry 会被直接提交；更早的 entry 则通过传递方式提交。如果两个日志具有相同的 index 和 term，那么分别应用它们截至该位置的内容必须产生相同的 LSM 状态。这些扩展将在第 4 节进行形式化证明。

**例 3.** 如图 3 所示，我们通过一个例子说明 LSM-Raft 的日志复制协议，其中涵盖 leader 提交位置推进、follower 同步、leader 选举和崩溃恢复。

**(a) 通过共享 LogSpan 推进提交位置。** 在这一阶段，Replica #4 刚刚完成压缩 SSTable L₁⁰ 的同步，该 SSTable 覆盖日志 span {4, 5}。由于此 SSTable 已被复制到达到 quorum 的节点上（例如副本 #2 和 #4），我们可以安全提交 index 4 和 5 处的 entry，并将 leader 的 commitIndex 从 2 推进到 5。

如果 leader 崩溃并开始新一轮选举，LSM-Raft 的选举机制仍与 Raft 一致：candidate 必须包含所有已提交 entry。Replica #2 持有截至 index 5 的原子 entry，因此符合条件。Replica #4 也符合条件，因为它的 L₁⁰ 在语义上覆盖 {4, 5}。

**(b) #4 成为 Leader：通过 LogSpan 重叠进行同步。** 现在假设 replica #4 成为新的 leader。它最后一个日志 index 为 6，并使用来自 L₁⁰ 的 preLogSpan = {4, 5} 发起复制。

- Replica #3 仅持有 entry #4，即 followerLogSpan = {4}，它是 leader span 的真子集。根据算法第 16 行，该 entry 被视为过时并将被删除。leader 重新发送 L₁⁰。
- Replica #5 仅持有 index 1，即 followerLogSpan = {1}，与 {4, 5} 不重叠。根据算法第 9 行，它拒绝此次复制尝试。leader 将 nextIndex 递减到 3，并更新 preLogSpan = {1, 2}。此时二者出现重叠（{1}），follower 会删除其 index 1 处的 entry，然后继续同步 index 3 及之后的 entry。

**(c) #2 成为 Leader：细粒度 Entry 同步。** 如果 replica #2 成为 leader，其 preLogSpan = {5}。

- Replica #4 持有 L₁⁰，且 followerLogSpan = {4, 5}。重叠部分包含 index 5，并且它与 #2 覆盖的最大 index 相同，因此根据算法第 13 行，leader 认为该 follower 已处于最新状态。
- 对 Replicas #3 和 #5 的处理与 (b) 相同：使用 followerLogSpan 进行比较，最终同步 index 更高的 entry。

**(d) #1 恢复：通过 SSTable 高效重新同步。** 在系统稳定运行一段时间后，先前崩溃的 replica #1 重启并重新加入集群。再次选举确认 #2 为 leader。由于 #1 已经过时，leader 根据算法第 23 行传输经过 compaction 的 SSTable L₂⁰；该 SSTable 在语义上覆盖全部缺失日志 entry。这样，replica #1 只需一次传输即可同步到最新状态，避免重复回放 entry，并降低恢复延迟和网络成本。

**图 3.** LSM-Raft 中的日志复制示例

LSM-Raft 不再基于精确的 index 和 term，而是通过 LogSpan 推理，对 Raft 的日志匹配原则进行了扩展。通过比较 leader 与 follower 的 span，不仅可以安全删除冗余 entry，并允许 follower 通过经过 compaction 的 SSTable 进行同步，还可以借助语义状态传输加速崩溃恢复。该机制显著降低了复制开销，提高了高写入场景下的收敛效率。

sections.title: 4 CORRECTNESS PROOF
sections.originalMarkdown: In this section, we present the correctness proof of our LSM-Raft protocol. Unlike the original Raft algorithm which models the state machine log as a strictly ordered sequence of atomic entries, our generalized model allows logs to evolve through compaction, batching, as long as the final state derived from these inputs remains consistent. We organize this section into three parts: state machine definition, property generalization, and formal verification via TLA+.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节给出 LSM-Raft 协议的正确性证明。原始 Raft 算法将状态机日志建模为严格有序的原子 entry 序列；与之不同，我们的广义模型允许日志通过 compaction 和 batching 演化，只要由这些输入得到的最终状态仍然一致即可。本节分为三部分：状态机定义、性质的广义化，以及通过 TLA+ 进行形式化验证。

sections.title: 4.1 LSM State Machine
sections.originalMarkdown:
We define the LSM State Funtion as the state machine’s final logical output, determined by emulating the behavior of an LSM-tree. The function mainly depends on log and the element order. Although the LSM settings like the SSTable thresholds of different levels in LSM-Tree also affects the final ouput, the different replicas should share the same settings and then we don’t consider it.

**Definition 3 (LSM State Function).** Given the log $\mathbf{L}$ and initial LSM State $\mathbf{S}$, the LSM state function generates the new state $\mathbf{S}'$.

$$\mathbf{S}' = \Phi(\mathbf{L}, \mathbf{S}) = \langle E'_1, E'_2, \ldots, E'_m \rangle.$$

where $E'_i = \cup\{E_j, E_{j+1}, \ldots\}$ and $E_j \in \mathbf{L} \cup \mathbf{S}$.

Here, $\Phi$ simulates the LSM-tree compaction process through ordered union operation $\cup$. It produces an ordered sequence of SSTables from the log $\mathbf{L}$.

**Example 4. Out-of-order Entries:** We assume SSTable size thresholds grow exponentially by level $[2,4,8,16,\ldots]$, and use the same setting in the following example. If data arrives in sequential order, such as $\langle[0], [1], [2]\rangle$, the state machine compacts it into SSTables as follows:

$$\Phi(\langle[0], [1], [2]\rangle, \emptyset) = \langle[0, 1], [2]\rangle$$

However, if the data arrives out of order, which is common in industrial scenarios, e.g., $\langle[0], [2], [1]\rangle$, then after inserting $[0]$ and $[2]$, the system flushes them together as $[0, 2]$. When $[1]$ arrives later, it is added separately. The resulting state becomes:

$$\Phi(\langle[0], [2], [1]\rangle, \emptyset) = \langle[0, 2], [1]\rangle$$

**Example 5. Duplicate Handling:** When a newly added element overlaps with previously ingested ones, the LSM compaction process retains the data from the higher-level SSTable. For example:

$$\Phi(\langle[0, 1, 2, 3], [4], [4, 5]\rangle, \langle[0]\rangle) = \langle[0, 1, 2, 3], [4, 5]\rangle$$

Here, the flushed SSTable $[0, 1, 2, 3]$ from level 2 overwrites the earlier entry $[0]$ from level 0 during compaction, since its level is higher. Similarly, $[4, 5]$ at level 1 dominates $[4]$ at level 0.

When duplicated records with the same key exist across log, we resolve conflicts by preferring the SSTable from higher levels. This strategy follows standard LSM-tree semantics. The rationale is twofold: 1. LSM-based databases typically adopt an append-only write model, where new data is appended sequentially and older entries are eventually compacted or merged. Thus, newer values naturally overwrite older ones during compaction. 2. In cases where updates to historical data do occur, such modifications are either written as newer entries or handled by auxiliary mechanisms. These updates are processed separately from normal compaction logic. As a result, resolving duplicate keys by retaining records from higher levels is both correct and efficient under common LSM design patterns.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们将 LSM State Function 定义为状态机的最终逻辑输出，该输出通过模拟 LSM-tree 的行为来确定。此函数主要取决于日志及其中元素的顺序。尽管 LSM 的设置（例如 LSM-tree 各层的 SSTable 阈值）也会影响最终输出，但不同副本应采用相同的设置，因此这里不予考虑。

**定义 3（LSM 状态函数）。** 给定日志 $\mathbf{L}$ 和初始 LSM 状态 $\mathbf{S}$，LSM 状态函数生成新状态 $\mathbf{S}'$。

$$\mathbf{S}' = \Phi(\mathbf{L}, \mathbf{S}) = \langle E'_1, E'_2, \ldots, E'_m \rangle.$$

其中，$E'_i = \cup\{E_j, E_{j+1}, \ldots\}$，且 $E_j \in \mathbf{L} \cup \mathbf{S}$。

这里，$\Phi$ 通过有序并操作 $\cup$ 模拟 LSM-tree 的 compaction 过程。它根据日志 $\mathbf{L}$ 生成一个有序的 SSTable 序列。

**示例 4. 乱序 Entry：** 我们假设 SSTable 的大小阈值随层级按指数增长，即 $[2,4,8,16,\ldots]$，并在下述示例中使用相同设置。如果数据按顺序到达，例如 $\langle[0], [1], [2]\rangle$，状态机将其 compact 成如下 SSTable：

$$\Phi(\langle[0], [1], [2]\rangle, \emptyset) = \langle[0, 1], [2]\rangle$$

但是，如果数据乱序到达（这种情况在工业场景中很常见），例如 $\langle[0], [2], [1]\rangle$，那么在插入 $[0]$ 和 $[2]$ 之后，系统会将它们一起 flush 为 $[0, 2]$。当 $[1]$ 随后到达时，它会被单独加入。最终状态变为：

$$\Phi(\langle[0], [2], [1]\rangle, \emptyset) = \langle[0, 2], [1]\rangle$$

**示例 5. 重复项处理：** 当新加入的元素与之前摄取的元素重叠时，LSM compaction 过程会保留来自更高层 SSTable 的数据。例如：

$$\Phi(\langle[0, 1, 2, 3], [4], [4, 5]\rangle, \langle[0]\rangle) = \langle[0, 1, 2, 3], [4, 5]\rangle$$

这里，来自 level 2、经 flush 得到的 SSTable $[0, 1, 2, 3]$ 在 compaction 期间会覆盖 level 0 中更早的 entry $[0]$，因为它所在的层级更高。类似地，level 1 的 $[4, 5]$ 优先于 level 0 的 $[4]$。

当日志中存在 key 相同的重复记录时，我们通过优先选择更高层的 SSTable 来解决冲突。这一策略遵循标准的 LSM-tree 语义。其理由有两点：1. 基于 LSM 的数据库通常采用 append-only 写入模型，新数据按顺序追加，旧 entry 最终会被 compact 或合并。因此，在 compaction 过程中，新值会自然覆盖旧值。2. 如果确实发生对历史数据的更新，这类修改要么作为更新的 entry 写入，要么由辅助机制处理。这些更新与常规 compaction 逻辑分开处理。因此，在常见的 LSM 设计模式下，通过保留更高层中的记录来解决重复 key，既正确又高效。

sections.title: 4.2 Property Generalization
sections.originalMarkdown:
To ensure that the generalized log semantics still preserve Raft’s guarantees, we revisit and adapt Raft’s five fundamental properties: Election Safety, Leader Append-Only, Log Matching, Leader Completeness, and State Machine Safety. In LSM-Raft, although log elements may overlap in their time ranges, these overlaps do not affect Election Safety and Leader Append-Only, as both are independent of log contents and rely solely on term and index ordering. We retain their standard definitions. The remaining three properties, however, require generalization to align with the semantics of the LSM-based state machine.

**Log Matching.** In traditional Raft, if two logs have the same entry at a given index and term, they must be identical in all previous entries. However, with the introduction of SSTables in LSM-Raft, this strict requirement is no longer applicable. Specifically, two logs with different elements could still yield the same LSM state. Therefore, we extend the condition to require that the final state—computed by the LSM state function $\Phi$—be consistent across replicas when log elements match in index and term. The generalized Log Matching as shown:

**Property 1. Log Matching with Generalization:** if two logs contain an element with the same index and term, then the states of applying all elements up through the given index are equivalent.

**Leader Completeness.** In Raft, once an entry is committed in a given term, it must appear in the logs of all future leaders. In LSM-Raft, we generalize this by requiring that all data points contained in a committed element must be included—possibly in compacted form—in future leader logs.

**Property 2. Leader Completeness with Generalization:** If a log element is committed in a given term, then that all the entries contained in the element will be present in the logs of the leaders for all higher-numbered terms.

If a log element $E_i$ is committed in term $t$, then for any higher term $t' > t$, every data entry $e \in E_i$ must be present in some element of the new leader’s log:

$$\forall e \in E_i \in \mathbf{L}_t,\ \exists E_j \in \mathbf{L}_{t'}\ \text{such that}\ e \in E_j$$

**State Machine Safety.** In the presence of asynchronous replication or compaction, some followers may have missing (null) elements at specific indices. When such a follower becomes a leader, it must ensure consistency by preserving the logical state derived from all non-null elements up to any given committed index. This guarantees eventual convergence, even with differing logs.

**Property 3. State Machine Safety with Generalization:** if a server has applied a log element at a given index to its state machine, no other server will ever apply a different log element for the same index.
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了确保广义日志语义仍然保留 Raft 的保证，我们重新审视并调整 Raft 的五项基本性质：Election Safety、Leader Append-Only、Log Matching、Leader Completeness 和 State Machine Safety。在 LSM-Raft 中，虽然日志元素的时间范围可能重叠，但这种重叠不会影响 Election Safety 和 Leader Append-Only，因为这两项性质与日志内容无关，只依赖 term 和 index 的顺序。因此，我们保留它们的标准定义。然而，其余三项性质需要进行广义化，以符合基于 LSM 的状态机语义。

**Log Matching。** 在传统 Raft 中，如果两份日志在给定 index 和 term 处具有相同的 entry，那么它们之前的所有 entry 都必须完全相同。然而，随着 LSM-Raft 引入 SSTable，这项严格要求不再适用。具体而言，包含不同元素的两份日志仍可能产生相同的 LSM 状态。因此，我们扩展了这一条件：当日志元素的 index 和 term 匹配时，由 LSM 状态函数 $\Phi$ 计算得到的最终状态必须在各副本之间保持一致。广义化后的 Log Matching 如下：

**性质 1. 广义 Log Matching：** 如果两份日志包含 index 和 term 均相同的元素，那么应用直到该 index 为止的所有元素后所得的状态等价。

**Leader Completeness。** 在 Raft 中，一旦某个 entry 在给定 term 中被提交，它就必须出现在所有未来 leader 的日志中。在 LSM-Raft 中，我们将其广义化为：已提交元素中包含的所有数据点都必须包含在未来 leader 的日志中，可能以 compact 后的形式存在。

**性质 2. 广义 Leader Completeness：** 如果一个日志元素在给定 term 中被提交，那么该元素包含的所有 entry 都会出现在所有更高编号 term 的 leader 日志中。

如果日志元素 $E_i$ 在 term $t$ 中被提交，那么对于任意更高的 term $t' > t$，每个数据 entry $e \in E_i$ 都必须出现在新 leader 日志的某个元素中：

$$\forall e \in E_i \in \mathbf{L}_t,\ \exists E_j \in \mathbf{L}_{t'}\ \text{使得}\ e \in E_j$$

**State Machine Safety。** 在存在异步复制或 compaction 的情况下，某些 follower 在特定 index 处可能缺失（即为 null）元素。当这样的 follower 成为 leader 时，它必须保留由截至任意给定已提交 index 的所有非 null 元素派生出的逻辑状态，从而确保一致性。即便日志不同，这也能保证最终收敛。

**性质 3. 广义 State Machine Safety：** 如果某个服务器已经将给定 index 处的日志元素应用到其状态机，则其他任何服务器都绝不会在同一 index 处应用不同的日志元素。

sections.title: 4.3 Formal Verification via TLA+
sections.titleTranslation: 4.3 通过 TLA+ 进行形式化验证
sections.originalMarkdown:
To ensure the correctness of LSM-Raft under the generalized log and state machine semantics, we model its behavior using TLA+ [20, 35]. Our goal is to prove that even when the log consists of entries and compacted SSTables, and the state machine follows LSM-tree semantics, Raft’s safety properties still hold.

```tla
-------- MODULE LSMRaft --------
EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences
CONSTANTS
Nodes, Terms, Entries, SSTables, InitialState
VARIABLES
log, committed, state
LogElem ==
[type : {"entry", "sstable"}, content : SUBSET Entries, term : Terms, index : Nat]
LSMState == Seq(SUBSET Entries)
LogPrefix(lg, i) == SubSeq(lg, 1, i)
Phi(logElems) ==
LET flatten == UNION { e.content : e \in logElems }
IN [flatten]
LogMatching ==
\A n1, n2 \in Nodes, i \in Nat :
/\ i <= Len(log[n1]) /\ i <= Len(log[n2])
/\ log[n1][i].index = log[n2][i].index
/\ log[n1][i].term = log[n2][i].term
=>
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n1], i)) =
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n2], i))
LeaderCompleteness ==
\A n1, n2 \in Nodes, i \in Nat :
/\ i <= committed[n1]
=>
\A e \in log[n1][i].content :
\E j \in 1..Len(log[n2]) :
e \in log[n2][j].content
StateMachineSafety ==
\A n1, n2 \in Nodes :
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n1], committed[n1])) =
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n2], committed[n2]))
```

We define the following components in our TLA+ specification. Firstly, a replicated log $\mathbf{L}$ as a sequence of log elements, where each element is either a singleton (entry) or a batch (SSTable). Secondly, the LSM state function $\Phi(\mathbf{L}, S_0)$, which deterministically computes the final state from a log and an initial state. Moreover, properties including Log Matching, Leader Completeness, and State Machine Safety, expressed as invariants over multiple replicas.

The TLA+ specification verifies that for any pair of replicas with matching logs up to a given index (modulo compaction), the resulting state machine outputs remain identical. Additionally, it ensures that once a log element is committed in a term, its effect appears in the logs of all subsequent leaders. A complete listing of our TLA+ module is provided in Appendix [9].
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了确保 LSM-Raft 在广义日志和状态机语义下的正确性，我们使用 TLA+ [20, 35] 对其行为进行建模。我们的目标是证明：即使日志由 entry 和 compact 后的 SSTable 组成，并且状态机遵循 LSM-tree 语义，Raft 的安全性质仍然成立。

```tla
-------- MODULE LSMRaft --------
EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences
CONSTANTS
Nodes, Terms, Entries, SSTables, InitialState
VARIABLES
log, committed, state
LogElem ==
[type : {"entry", "sstable"}, content : SUBSET Entries, term : Terms, index : Nat]
LSMState == Seq(SUBSET Entries)
LogPrefix(lg, i) == SubSeq(lg, 1, i)
Phi(logElems) ==
LET flatten == UNION { e.content : e \in logElems }
IN [flatten]
LogMatching ==
\A n1, n2 \in Nodes, i \in Nat :
/\ i <= Len(log[n1]) /\ i <= Len(log[n2])
/\ log[n1][i].index = log[n2][i].index
/\ log[n1][i].term = log[n2][i].term
=>
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n1], i)) =
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n2], i))
LeaderCompleteness ==
\A n1, n2 \in Nodes, i \in Nat :
/\ i <= committed[n1]
=>
\A e \in log[n1][i].content :
\E j \in 1..Len(log[n2]) :
e \in log[n2][j].content
StateMachineSafety ==
\A n1, n2 \in Nodes :
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n1], committed[n1])) =
Phi(LogPrefix(log[n2], committed[n2]))
```

我们在 TLA+ 规约中定义了以下组件。首先，将复制日志 $\mathbf{L}$ 定义为日志元素序列，其中每个元素要么是单个元素（entry），要么是一个批次（SSTable）。其次，定义 LSM 状态函数 $\Phi(\mathbf{L}, S_0)$，它根据日志和初始状态确定性地计算最终状态。此外，还将 Log Matching、Leader Completeness 和 State Machine Safety 等性质表示为多个副本上的不变量。

该 TLA+ 规约验证了：对于任意一对日志在给定 index 之前匹配（考虑 compaction 等价）的副本，所得状态机输出保持相同。此外，它还确保日志元素一旦在某个 term 中被提交，其效果就会出现在所有后续 leader 的日志中。附录 [9] 给出了完整的 TLA+ 模块清单。

sections.title: 5 LEVEL DETERMINATION MODEL
sections.originalMarkdown:
In the traditional Raft log replication protocol, the leader must transmit each log entry one by one to all follower nodes. However, in real-world systems, some follower nodes may experience synchronization delays due to network congestion or local I/O pressure. This leads to log accumulation on the leader, which impacts write confirmation latency and overall system throughput.

LSM-Raft addresses this problem by proposing an optimization: when a follower falls significantly behind, the leader can transmit a compressed, higher-level SSTable to replace multiple pending raw log entries. This reduces the number of transmission rounds and volume of transferred data, while also relieving the follower of unnecessary compaction overhead.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在传统 Raft 日志复制协议中，leader 必须将每条日志 entry 逐一发送给所有 follower 节点。然而在现实系统中，某些 follower 节点可能因网络拥塞或本地 I/O 压力而出现同步延迟。这会导致日志在 leader 上积压，进而影响写确认延迟和系统整体吞吐量。

LSM-Raft 提出了一项优化来解决这个问题：当某个 follower 严重落后时，leader 可以发送一个经过压缩的高层 SSTable，以替代多条待发送的原始日志 entry。这样既减少了传输轮数和数据传输量，也免除了 follower 不必要的 compaction 开销。

sections.title: 5.1 Definition of Log Equivalence
sections.originalMarkdown:
We first introduce the notion of log equivalence, which determines whether two log sequences result in the same state for a given state machine.

**Definition 4 (Log Eqivalence).** Let $\mathbf{L}_1$ and $\mathbf{L}_2$ be two log sequences, and $\mathbf{S}_f$ be the current state of a follower. Let $\Phi(\cdot, \cdot)$ be the state function representing the result of applying logs to the LSM-based state machine. If

$$\Phi(\mathbf{L}_1, \mathbf{S}_f) = \Phi(\mathbf{L}_2, \mathbf{S}_f) \tag{1}$$

then $\mathbf{L}_1$ and $\mathbf{L}_2$ are semantically equivalent under $\mathbf{S}_f$, denoted as:

$$\mathbf{L}_1 \overset{\mathbf{S}_f}{\equiv} \mathbf{L}_2 \tag{2}$$

This equivalence relation allows us to search for lower-cost log variants without compromising final state consistency. It is crucial for LSM-Raft optimization because it guarantees that state machine safaty is maintained, even when the log structure is altered for efficiency.

**Example 6.** Consider a Raft cluster with 5 nodes. Each write batch contains 1000 records, and the SSTable size is 10000. After ingesting data $[19001, 20000]$, a level-1 SSTable (index=20) is created and successfully committed by followers 1 and 2. Follower 3, however, is still trying to receive $[10001, 11000]$ of index 11, and also the queued logs from index 12 to 20. A new SSTable covering $[10001, 20000]$ is now available. Let $\mathbf{L}_{v20} = \langle[10001, 11000], \ldots, [19001, 20000]\rangle$ and $E_{sst} = [10001, 20000]$, it easy to derive that $\mathbf{L}_{v20}$ and $\langle E_{sst}\rangle$ is log equivalent. This indicates that sending just $E_{sst}$ instead of all ten original log entries preserves the same final state.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们首先引入日志等价性的概念，用于判断两个日志序列对于给定状态机是否会产生相同状态。

**定义 4（日志等价性）。** 设 $\mathbf{L}_1$ 和 $\mathbf{L}_2$ 为两个日志序列，$\mathbf{S}_f$ 为 follower 的当前状态。设 $\Phi(\cdot, \cdot)$ 为状态函数，表示将日志应用于基于 LSM 的状态机后得到的结果。如果

$$\Phi(\mathbf{L}_1, \mathbf{S}_f) = \Phi(\mathbf{L}_2, \mathbf{S}_f) \tag{1}$$

那么 $\mathbf{L}_1$ 和 $\mathbf{L}_2$ 在 $\mathbf{S}_f$ 下语义等价，记为：

$$\mathbf{L}_1 \overset{\mathbf{S}_f}{\equiv} \mathbf{L}_2 \tag{2}$$

这种等价关系使我们能够在不破坏最终状态一致性的前提下，搜索成本更低的日志变体。它对于 LSM-Raft 的优化至关重要，因为它保证了即使为了提高效率而改变日志结构，State Machine Safety 仍能得到维护。

**示例 6.** 考虑一个包含 5 个节点的 Raft 集群。每个 write batch 包含 1000 条记录，SSTable 大小为 10000。摄取数据 $[19001, 20000]$ 后，系统创建了一个 level-1 SSTable（index=20），并由 follower 1 和 follower 2 成功提交。然而，follower 3 仍在尝试接收 index 11 的 $[10001, 11000]$，以及排队等待的 index 12 至 20 的日志。此时，一个覆盖 $[10001, 20000]$ 的新 SSTable 已经可用。令 $\mathbf{L}_{v20} = \langle[10001, 11000], \ldots, [19001, 20000]\rangle$，且 $E_{sst} = [10001, 20000]$，很容易推导出 $\mathbf{L}_{v20}$ 与 $\langle E_{sst}\rangle$ 日志等价。这说明，只发送 $E_{sst}$ 而不是全部十条原始日志 entry，仍能保持相同的最终状态。

sections.title: 5.2 Cost Modeling for Log Synchronization
sections.originalMarkdown:
We define a cost function $C(\cdot)$ over the space of log sequences. This function quantifies the overhead incurred when synchronizing logs rom leader to follower. For any log sequence $\mathbf{L}$, the total cost is decomposed as:

$$C(\mathbf{L}) = \sum_{C_i \in \mathcal{D}_C} C_i(\mathbf{L})$$

We focus on two dominant contributors: *transmission cost* and *compaction cost*. Transmission cost reflects the bandwidth and transmission round complexity required to send the logs to the follower. It includes: the sum of entry sizes $|E|$ for raw entries, the compressed size for SSTables ($\rho$ is the compression ratio), and a per-element transmission overhead $\delta$. $\mathbf{L}^{ent}_f$, $\mathbf{L}^{sst}_f$ denotes the set of singleton entries and compacted SSTables.

**Fig. 4. Simple Example for Level Determination in LSM-Raft.** The leader maintains a multi-level LSM-tree containing both atomic entries and compacted SSTables across levels $L_0$, $L_1$, and $L_2$. When synchronizing with followers, the system determines the optimal SSTable transmission strategy by evaluating the transmission-compaction cost $\Delta(C_{tran}, C_{comp})$. Each arrow between the leader and follower indicates a potential transmission decision for a selected SSTable, along with its associated cost pair. For already cost, we have transmits about assuming the weights for compaction overhead per level are $\{1, 2, 4\}$ respectively. Follower 1 evaluates five possible options, each with a different combination of transmission and expected local compaction cost. The optimal combination $(3 \times 1 + 2 \times 2, 2 \times 1 + 4 \times 1)$ results in a total cost $(7, 6)$, indicating transmission size 7 and local compaction overhead 6 units. This selection minimizes overall effort compared to other alternatives such as $(8\rho, 0)$, which defers compaction entirely to the leader.

$$C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}_f) = \sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{ent}_f} (|E| + \delta) + \sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{sst}_f} (\rho|E| + \delta) \tag{3}$$

$$= \sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{ent}_f}|E| + \rho\sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{sst}_f}|E| + |\mathbf{L}_f|\delta \tag{4}$$

*Compaction cost* captures the effort of incorporating logs into the local LSM-Tree. For each element $E_i$, the cost depends on the difference between the target LSM-Tree level and the original one.

$$C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}_f) = \sum_{E_i \in \mathbf{L}_f}\left(\operatorname{Level}(\Phi(\mathbf{L}_f, \mathbf{S}_f)) - \operatorname{Level}(E_i)\right) \cdot |E_i| \tag{5}$$

where the Level denotes the corresponding LSM-Tree level of the given states or elements. This reflects that compaction costs are higher when moving from lower to higher levels.

Overall cost is the sum of both costs for simplicity, $C^*(\mathbf{L}_f) = C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}_f) + C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}_f)$. This formula provides a unified metric to guide replication decisions.

**Example 7.** Following the Example 6, we assume $\rho = 0.5$, $\delta = 10$, and $|E| = 1000$. For $\mathbf{L}_{v20}$ with 10 raw log elements:

$$C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}_{v20}) = 10 \times 1000 + 10 \times 10 = 10100$$

$$C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}_{v20}) = 10 \times (1 - 0) \times 1000 = 10000$$

For $\langle E_{sst}\rangle$ of size 10000:

$$C_{trans}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 0.5 \times 10000 + 10 = 5010$$

$$C_{comp}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 0$$

To formalize this with weight preference $\alpha = 1$, $beta = 0.1$

$$C^*(\mathbf{L}_{v20}) = 10100 + 0.1 \times 10000 = 11100$$

$$C^*(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 5010$$

The overall cost comparison clearly favors $E_{sst}$. This demonstrates that compaction-aware, compression-optimized SSTable transfer achieves significantly lower overhead.

Compared with Example 4 and 5, Exmample 7 shows that when the index range is enlarged to 10,000, the overall cost is also reduced significantly (from 11,100 to 5,010), indicating improvement.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们在日志序列空间上定义成本函数 $C(\cdot)$。该函数量化将日志从 leader 同步到 follower 时产生的开销。对于任意日志序列 $\mathbf{L}$，总成本分解为：

$$C(\mathbf{L}) = \sum_{C_i \in \mathcal{D}_C} C_i(\mathbf{L})$$

我们重点考虑两个主要因素：*传输成本*和 *compaction 成本*。传输成本反映将日志发送给 follower 所需的带宽和传输轮次复杂度。它包括：原始 entry 的大小之和 $|E|$、SSTable 的压缩后大小（$\rho$ 为压缩率），以及每个元素的传输开销 $\delta$。$\mathbf{L}^{ent}_f$、$\mathbf{L}^{sst}_f$ 分别表示单个 entry 和 compact 后 SSTable 的集合。

**图 4. LSM-Raft 中层级确定的简单示例。** Leader 维护一棵多层 LSM-tree，其中在 $L_0$、$L_1$ 和 $L_2$ 各层同时包含原子 entry 和 compact 后的 SSTable。与 follower 同步时，系统通过评估传输-compaction 成本 $\Delta(C_{tran}, C_{comp})$ 来确定最优的 SSTable 传输策略。Leader 与 follower 之间的每条箭头都表示针对所选 SSTable 的一种潜在传输决策，以及与之对应的一对成本。对于已有成本，我们假设各层 compaction 开销的权重分别为 $\{1, 2, 4\}$ 来讨论传输。Follower 1 评估五种可能方案，每种方案对应传输成本和预期本地 compaction 成本的不同组合。最优组合 $(3 \times 1 + 2 \times 2, 2 \times 1 + 4 \times 1)$ 得到总成本 $(7, 6)$，表示传输大小为 7，本地 compaction 开销为 6 个单位。与 $(8\rho, 0)$ 等其他备选方案相比，该选择使整体工作量最小；后者将 compaction 完全留给 leader。

$$C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}_f) = \sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{ent}_f} (|E| + \delta) + \sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{sst}_f} (\rho|E| + \delta) \tag{3}$$

$$= \sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{ent}_f}|E| + \rho\sum_{E \in \mathbf{L}^{sst}_f}|E| + |\mathbf{L}_f|\delta \tag{4}$$

*Compaction 成本*刻画将日志纳入本地 LSM-tree 的工作量。对于每个元素 $E_i$，其成本取决于目标 LSM-tree 层级与原始层级之差。

$$C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}_f) = \sum_{E_i \in \mathbf{L}_f}\left(\operatorname{Level}(\Phi(\mathbf{L}_f, \mathbf{S}_f)) - \operatorname{Level}(E_i)\right) \cdot |E_i| \tag{5}$$

其中，Level 表示给定状态或元素对应的 LSM-tree 层级。这反映了从较低层移动到较高层时 compaction 成本更高。

为简单起见，总成本取两类成本之和，即 $C^*(\mathbf{L}_f) = C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}_f) + C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}_f)$。该公式提供了一个指导复制决策的统一指标。

**示例 7.** 沿用示例 6，假设 $\rho = 0.5$、$\delta = 10$，且 $|E| = 1000$。对于包含 10 个原始日志元素的 $\mathbf{L}_{v20}$：

$$C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}_{v20}) = 10 \times 1000 + 10 \times 10 = 10100$$

$$C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}_{v20}) = 10 \times (1 - 0) \times 1000 = 10000$$

对于大小为 10000 的 $\langle E_{sst}\rangle$：

$$C_{trans}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 0.5 \times 10000 + 10 = 5010$$

$$C_{comp}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 0$$

使用权重偏好 $\alpha = 1$、$beta = 0.1$ 将其形式化为：

$$C^*(\mathbf{L}_{v20}) = 10100 + 0.1 \times 10000 = 11100$$

$$C^*(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 5010$$

总体成本比较显然更倾向于 $E_{sst}$。这表明，同时考虑 compaction 且针对压缩进行优化的 SSTable 传输可以显著降低开销。

与示例 4 和示例 5 相比，示例 7 表明，当 index 范围扩大到 10,000 时，总体成本也显著降低（从 11,100 降至 5,010），说明性能得到改善。

sections.title: 5.3 Optimization Objective and Strategy
sections.originalMarkdown:
Given a follower’s current log $\mathbf{L}_f$ and a newly flushed SSTable $E^{sst}$, we define a candidate update $\mathbf{L}'_f = \mathbf{L}_f \cup \langle E^{sst}\rangle$. To select the best option, we consider all semantically equivalent log forms $\mathcal{E}_{\mathbf{L}_f}$ and select:

$$\mathbf{L}^* = \underset{\mathbf{L} \in \mathcal{E}_{\mathbf{L}_f}}{\arg\min}\ C^*(\mathbf{L}) \tag{6}$$

**Example 8.** Follower 4 is at index 18, last received $[15001, 16000]$. A new SSTable covering $[16001, 20000]$ is available, then the log $\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4} = \langle[17001, 18000], [18001, 19000], [19001, 20000]\rangle$. Assume $|E| = 1000$, $\rho = 0.5$, $\delta = 10$, all entries at level 0. Original cost and SSTable cost is as shown.

$$C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4}) = 3030, \qquad C_{trans}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 5010$$

$$C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4}) = 10000, \qquad C_{comp}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 0$$

$$C^*(\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4}) = 4030, \qquad C^*(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 5010$$

Hence, the original log yields lower total cost and should be used.

This strategy enables intelligent decisions when transmitting SSTables, improving replication throughput without sacrificing consistency. It is particularly beneficial for time-series or high-frequency ingest workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown:
给定 follower 的当前日志 $\mathbf{L}_f$ 和一个新 flush 的 SSTable $E^{sst}$，我们定义候选更新 $\mathbf{L}'_f = \mathbf{L}_f \cup \langle E^{sst}\rangle$。为了选择最佳方案，我们考虑所有语义等价的日志形式 $\mathcal{E}_{\mathbf{L}_f}$，并选择：

$$\mathbf{L}^* = \underset{\mathbf{L} \in \mathcal{E}_{\mathbf{L}_f}}{\arg\min}\ C^*(\mathbf{L}) \tag{6}$$

**示例 8.** Follower 4 当前位于 index 18，最后接收到的是 $[15001, 16000]$。此时有一个覆盖 $[16001, 20000]$ 的新 SSTable 可用，因此日志 $\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4} = \langle[17001, 18000], [18001, 19000], [19001, 20000]\rangle$。假设 $|E| = 1000$、$\rho = 0.5$、$\delta = 10$，且所有 entry 均位于 level 0。原始成本与 SSTable 成本如下所示。

$$C_{trans}(\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4}) = 3030, \qquad C_{trans}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 5010$$

$$C_{comp}(\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4}) = 10000, \qquad C_{comp}(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 0$$

$$C^*(\mathbf{L}^{v20}_{f4}) = 4030, \qquad C^*(\langle E_{sst}\rangle) = 5010$$

因此，原始日志的总成本更低，应当使用原始日志。

该策略使系统能够在传输 SSTable 时做出智能决策，在不牺牲一致性的情况下提高复制吞吐量。它对时间序列或高频摄取 workload 尤其有益。

sections.title: 5.4 SSTable Replacement Decision Algorithm
sections.originalMarkdown: To efficiently decide whether to replace a series of raw log entries with a compressed SSTable, we introduce a lightweight, cost-delta-based decision algorithm. Instead of simulating the entire state machine, this method compares only the total transmission and compaction costs between the original log and the candidate SSTable. The principle is straightforward: If the cost of sending the SSTable is lower than that of sending and compacting the raw log entries it overlaps with, the SSTable should be chosen.
sections.translationMarkdown: 为了高效判断是否应使用压缩后的 SSTable 替换一系列原始日志 entry，我们引入了一种基于成本差值的轻量级决策算法。该方法不模拟整个状态机，而只比较原始日志与候选 SSTable 的传输和 compaction 总成本。其原理很直接：如果发送 SSTable 的成本低于发送并 compact 与其重叠的原始日志 entry 的成本，就应选择 SSTable。

sections.title: 5.4.1 Algorithm Complexity
sections.originalMarkdown:
Let $\mathbf{L}_f$ be the current follower’s pending log entries, and $E_{sst}$ a newly flushed SSTable overlapping part of $\mathbf{L}_f$. $r_{log}$ $r_{sst}$, $o$ ,$s$ are defined as shown in the Algorithm 2. The cost differences are computed as Lines 5 and 6 indicate.

If $\Delta C < 0$, then transmitting $E_{sst}$ is considered more efficient as Line 8 shows. Otherwise, the original raw entries are chosen. This evaluation method is both fast and effective. It relies solely on metadata like timestamp ranges and record counts, and avoids heavyweight computation such as full LSM compaction simulation. Algorithm 2 involves only constant-time operations, including calculating ranges, overlaps, and costs. Both time and space complexity are $O(1)$, making the algorithm highly suitable for real-time usage in replication-intensive environments.

**Algorithm 2 SSTableDecision**

**Require:** Log sequence $\mathbf{L}_f$, SSTable $E_{sst}$, compression ratio $\rho$, transmission overhead $\delta$

**Ensure:** `True` if SSTable is preferable, otherwise `False`

```text
 1: r_log ← timestamp range of L_f
 2: r_sst ← timestamp range of E_sst
 3: o ← overlap length of r_log and r_sst
 4: s ← |E_sst|                         ▷ Number of records in SSTable
 5: ΔC_trans ← o − ρ · s
 6: ΔC_comp ← −s
 7: ΔC ← ΔC_trans + ΔC_comp
 8: if ΔC < 0 then
 9:     return True
10: else
11:     return False
12: end if
```
sections.translationMarkdown:
设 $\mathbf{L}_f$ 为当前 follower 待处理的日志 entry，$E_{sst}$ 为一个新 flush 的 SSTable，且它与 $\mathbf{L}_f$ 的一部分重叠。$r_{log}$、$r_{sst}$、$o$、$s$ 的定义如算法 2 所示。成本差值按照第 5 行和第 6 行计算。

如果 $\Delta C < 0$，则如第 8 行所示，传输 $E_{sst}$ 被认为效率更高。否则，选择原始的 raw entry。这种评估方法快速且有效。它只依赖时间戳范围和记录数等 metadata，避免了完整 LSM compaction 模拟之类的重量级计算。算法 2 只涉及常数时间操作，包括计算范围、重叠长度和成本。因此其时间复杂度和空间复杂度均为 $O(1)$，非常适合在复制密集型环境中实时使用。

**算法 2 SSTableDecision**

**输入要求：** 日志序列 $\mathbf{L}_f$、SSTable $E_{sst}$、压缩率 $\rho$、传输开销 $\delta$

**输出保证：** 如果 SSTable 更优，则返回 `True`；否则返回 `False`

```text
 1: r_log ← L_f 的时间戳范围
 2: r_sst ← E_sst 的时间戳范围
 3: o ← r_log 与 r_sst 的重叠长度
 4: s ← |E_sst|                         ▷ SSTable 中的记录数
 5: ΔC_trans ← o − ρ · s
 6: ΔC_comp ← −s
 7: ΔC ← ΔC_trans + ΔC_comp
 8: if ΔC < 0 then
 9:     return True
10: else
11:     return False
12: end if
```

sections.title: 5.4.2 Query Correctness
sections.originalMarkdown:
We explain that the query of the client’s requests are still correct by skipping low level SSTable after applying Algorithm 2.

**Consistency Despite Missing Lower-Level SSTables:** As shown in Figure 4, missing a lower-level SSTable does not affect consistency. If a follower receives $L_{i+1}$ but not $L_i$, three cases are possible: (A) $L_{i+1}$ is generated from $L_i$ and others—equivalence holds; (B) $L_{i+1}$ is newer—$L_i$’s data is preserved elsewhere; (C) $L_{i+1}$ is older—$L_i$ will be transmitted later. In all cases, the follower retains a consistent view of the committed data.

LSM-Raft adopts the standard Raft leader-based read model to ensure query correctness. As long as a majority of replicas have committed the log, client queries—served by the leader—are guaranteed to return the latest committed data. Thus, query results remain correct and unaffected by the state of lower-level SSTables.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们说明，在应用算法 2 后跳过低层 SSTable，客户端请求的查询仍然是正确的。

**缺失低层 SSTable 时仍保持一致性：** 如图 4 所示，缺失一个低层 SSTable 不会影响一致性。如果 follower 接收到 $L_{i+1}$ 而没有接收到 $L_i$，可能存在三种情况：(A) $L_{i+1}$ 由 $L_i$ 和其他数据生成，此时等价性成立；(B) $L_{i+1}$ 更新，$L_i$ 的数据保存在其他位置；(C) $L_{i+1}$ 更旧，$L_i$ 将在稍后传输。在所有情况下，follower 都能保持对已提交数据的一致视图。

LSM-Raft 采用标准的、基于 Raft leader 的读取模型来确保查询正确性。只要多数副本已经提交日志，由 leader 服务的客户端查询就能保证返回最新的已提交数据。因此，查询结果仍然正确，不受低层 SSTable 状态的影响。

sections.title: 6 IMPLEMENTATION
sections.originalMarkdown: This section presents the system implementation of LSM-Raft, focusing on how the design principles are realized across the WAL, consensus, and storage layers. The architecture integrates the LSM-tree compaction model into a Raft-style consensus protocol while ensuring log consistency and fault tolerance.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节介绍 LSM-Raft 的系统实现，重点说明如何在 WAL、共识和存储各层中落实其设计原则。该架构将 LSM-tree compaction 模型集成到 Raft 风格的共识协议中，同时保证日志一致性和容错能力。

sections.title: 6.1 System Overview
sections.originalMarkdown:
Figure 5 illustrates the architecture of LSM-Raft. The system is composed of three primary layers: WAL Layer is Responsible for capturing incoming write requests from clients and ensuring persistence before replication. Consensus Layer handles log agreement across nodes and decides whether to replicate entries or compressed SSTables. Storage Layer maintains an LSM-tree-based storage engine and supports compaction and SSTable merging. We integrate LSM-Raft into two open-source database systems—TiDB [22] and Apache IoTDB [2, 41]—across the WAL, consensus, and storage.

*Fig. 5. The System Implementation of LSM-Raft*
sections.translationMarkdown:
图 5 展示了 LSM-Raft 的架构。系统由三个主要层次组成：WAL 层负责捕获来自客户端的传入写请求，并确保在复制之前将其持久化。共识层处理节点间的日志一致，并决定复制原始 entry 还是压缩后的 SSTable。存储层维护基于 LSM-tree 的存储引擎，并支持 compaction 和 SSTable 合并。我们在 WAL、共识和存储层将 LSM-Raft 集成到了两个开源数据库系统中，即 TiDB [22] 和 Apache IoTDB [2, 41]。

*图 5. LSM-Raft 的系统实现*

sections.title: 6.2 Consensus Layer: SSTable Decision
sections.originalMarkdown:
A dedicated Log Dispatcher is responsible for feeding newly arrived log entries into the consensus layer. Depending on system state and follower lag, the dispatcher may either pass raw entries or trigger compaction to form SSTables for transmission.

The consensus layer is enhanced with a policy module that decides, for each follower, whether to replicate raw entries or compacted SSTables. This decision is based on the Algorithm 2 with then maintained `nextIndex` and `matchIndex`. In TiDB, we modify the RaftStore module to support generalized log element management. In IoTDB, we extend its built-in consensus module (PipeConsensus) to support log element switching.
sections.translationMarkdown:
一个专用的 Log Dispatcher 负责将新到达的 log entry 送入共识层。根据系统状态和 follower 的落后程度，dispatcher 可以传递原始 entry，也可以触发 compaction 以形成用于传输的 SSTable。

我们在共识层中加入了一个策略模块，用于针对每个 follower 决定复制原始 entry 还是经过 compaction 的 SSTable。该决策基于算法 2，并结合随后维护的 `nextIndex` 和 `matchIndex`。在 TiDB 中，我们修改 RaftStore 模块以支持广义日志元素管理。在 IoTDB 中，我们扩展其内置共识模块 PipeConsensus，以支持日志元素切换。

sections.title: 6.3 Storage Layer and LSM Integration
sections.titleTranslation: 6.3 存储层与 LSM 集成
sections.originalMarkdown:
At both leader and follower nodes, the local storage engine is built upon an LSM-tree. Once an entry or SSTable is deemed committed, it is flushed into the local LSM-tree through standard compaction procedures.

Each SSTable includes metadata identifying its compaction level, log index, and term, which is used during replication to ensure correctness. The compaction process at the leader is also reused to generate transferable SSTables that help reduce synchronization overhead. TiDB (via TiKV) uses RocksDB as its LSM-based storage engine, while IoTDB adopts an LSM-like storage engine based on the TsFile format. We adapt their compaction pipelines to support metadata-aware SSTable replay and verification.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在 leader 和 follower 节点上，本地存储引擎都构建于 LSM-tree 之上。一旦某个 entry 或 SSTable 被判定为已提交，它就会通过标准 compaction 流程被刷入本地 LSM-tree。

每个 SSTable 都包含用于标识其 compaction level、log index 和 term 的 metadata，这些信息在复制过程中用于确保正确性。leader 上的 compaction 过程也会被复用，以生成可传输的 SSTable，从而帮助降低同步开销。TiDB 通过 TiKV 使用 RocksDB 作为其基于 LSM 的存储引擎，而 IoTDB 则采用基于 TsFile 格式的类 LSM 存储引擎。我们调整了二者的 compaction pipeline，使其支持感知 metadata 的 SSTable replay 和 verification。

sections.title: 6.4 Failure Recovery and Log Convergence
sections.originalMarkdown:
When a follower node crashes and later recovers, it resumes from its latest durable WAL state. The leader uses the `nextIndex` pointer to retry replication. Depending on the follower’s current log span, the leader can choose to resend entries, trigger SSTable re-transmission, or compact overlapping ranges, as indicated in Algorithm 1.

Log convergence is ensured through the AppendEntries protocol: inconsistent logs are pruned and replaced based on log span conflict detection. The monotonicity of `matchIndex` and quorum-based commit logic guarantees safety despite compaction and transmission optimization. Failure recovery in TiDB and IoTDB reuses the native WAL replay mechanisms, with additional logic for element-level consistency checking.
sections.translationMarkdown:
当 follower 节点崩溃并随后恢复时，它会从最近的持久化 WAL 状态继续运行。leader 使用 `nextIndex` 指针重试复制。如算法 1 所示，根据 follower 当前的日志跨度，leader 可以选择重新发送 entry、触发 SSTable 重传或对重叠范围执行 compaction。

日志收敛由 AppendEntries 协议保证：系统根据日志跨度冲突检测，裁剪并替换不一致的日志。尽管采用了 compaction 和传输优化，`matchIndex` 的单调性以及基于 quorum 的提交逻辑仍能保证安全性。TiDB 和 IoTDB 中的故障恢复复用了原生 WAL replay 机制，并增加了用于元素级一致性检查的逻辑。

sections.title: 7 EXPERIMENTS
sections.originalMarkdown: This section evaluates the performance, efficiency, and fault tolerance of LSM-Raft through a series of experiments. We compare LSM-Raft against traditional Raft across various datasets and workload conditions to demonstrate its advantages in throughput and resource usage.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节通过一系列实验评估 LSM-Raft 的性能、效率和容错能力。我们在多种数据集和 workload 条件下将 LSM-Raft 与传统 Raft 进行比较，以展示其在吞吐量和资源使用方面的优势。

sections.title: 7.1 Setup
sections.originalMarkdown:
We evaluate two representative systems: TiDB [7, 22], designed for transactional and mixed OLTP/OLAP workloads, and Apache IoTDB [1, 41], targeting time-series ingestion workloads. Implementation and integration details of LSM-Raft within both systems are presented in Section 6.

Unless otherwise specified, we use the default configuration settings (e.g., an SSTable size of 10,000) for each database.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们评估了两个有代表性的系统：面向事务型和混合 OLTP/OLAP workload 的 TiDB [7, 22]，以及面向时间序列摄取 workload 的 Apache IoTDB [1, 41]。LSM-Raft 在这两个系统中的实现和集成细节见第 6 节。

除非另有说明，我们对每个数据库均采用默认配置，例如将 SSTable 大小设置为 10,000。

sections.title: 7.1.1 Datasets
sections.originalMarkdown:
Four representative datasets, BoTIoT [26], EdgeIIoT [19], ToNIoT [10], CitiBike [17] are chosen for experiments. They are widely adopted for evaluating intrusion detection systems and distributed data analytics with an emphasis on their data sources, record scale, feature dimensions.

Table 2 summarizes key statistics of the above datasets.

*Table 2. Datasets Statistics*

| Dataset | Year | Records | Features |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| BoTIoT [26] | 2019 | > 72M | Dozens |
| EdgeIIoT [19] | 2022 | ∼ 21M | 61 |
| ToNIoT [10] | 2020 | ∼30k/device | Varies |
| CitiBike [17] | 2013-2025 | > 300M | 15+ |
sections.translationMarkdown:
实验选择了四个有代表性的数据集：BoTIoT [26]、EdgeIIoT [19]、ToNIoT [10] 和 CitiBike [17]。这些数据集被广泛用于评估入侵检测系统和分布式数据分析，重点体现其数据来源、记录规模和特征维度。

表 2 汇总了上述数据集的关键统计信息。

*表 2. 数据集统计信息*

| 数据集 | 年份 | 记录数 | 特征数 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| BoTIoT [26] | 2019 | > 72M | 数十个 |
| EdgeIIoT [19] | 2022 | ∼ 21M | 61 |
| ToNIoT [10] | 2020 | ∼30k/device | 不固定 |
| CitiBike [17] | 2013-2025 | > 300M | 15+ |

sections.title: 7.1.2 Benchmark Workload
sections.originalMarkdown:
To demonstrate practical impact with realistic deployment scenarios from production, workloads are generated using two established benchmarking frameworks: YCSB [8] for TiDB and iot-benchmark [6] for IoTDB. For instance, Apache IoTDB is deployed in the urban rail vehicle monitoring system where each train is equipped with tens of thousands of sensors - such large-scale workloads can be accessed using iot-benchmark. Here, LSM-Raft’s ability to cut overhead and adaptively reduce redundant data transmission can significantly improve system responsiveness (about 5%-9% in Figures 6) and resource efficiency (over 30% reduction in Figures 11, 12, 13).

To evaluate performance across a range of ingestion intensities, we vary the number of concurrent clients from 1 (light-ingestion) to 1024 (heavy-ingestion). Clients continuously issue append-heavy write operations targeted at the leader node, which replicates all writes synchronously to follower replicas.
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了在来自生产环境的真实部署场景中展示实际效果，我们使用两个成熟的基准测试框架生成 workload：TiDB 使用 YCSB [8]，IoTDB 使用 iot-benchmark [6]。例如，Apache IoTDB 被部署在城市轨道交通车辆监控系统中，每列列车配备数万个传感器；这类大规模 workload 可以使用 iot-benchmark 进行访问。在此场景下，LSM-Raft 降低开销并自适应减少冗余数据传输的能力，可以显著改善系统响应能力（图 6 中约为 5%-9%）和资源效率（图 11、12、13 中降低超过 30%）。

为了评估不同摄取强度下的性能，我们将并发客户端数量从 1（轻量摄取）调整到 1024（重度摄取）。客户端持续向 leader 节点发出以 append 为主的写操作，leader 会将所有写入同步复制到 follower replica。

sections.title: 7.1.3 Evaluation Metrics
sections.originalMarkdown: We use several key metrics to assess system performance.
