System throughput (points/sec) measures the number of data points processed per second, reflecting the system’s overall data ingestion capability. CPU utilization monitors the resource usage of each server node under varying workloads. Compaction cost records the number of data points involved in the compaction process, reflecting the overhead introduced by internal data merging. Network cost measures the bandwidth consumed during data replication and synchronization across servers, representing the communication overhead of the consensus protocol.
sections.translationMarkdown: 我们使用若干关键指标评估系统性能。系统吞吐量（points/sec）衡量每秒处理的数据点数量，反映系统的整体数据摄取能力。CPU 利用率监控各服务器节点在不同 workload 下的资源使用情况。Compaction cost 记录参与 compaction 过程的数据点数量，反映内部数据合并引入的开销。Network cost 衡量服务器间数据复制和同步所消耗的带宽，代表共识协议的通信开销。

sections.title: 7.2 Overall Throughput
sections.originalMarkdown:
*Fig. 6. System throughput in IoTDB.*

*Fig. 7. System throughput in TiDB.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
*图 6. IoTDB 中的系统吞吐量。*

*图 7. TiDB 中的系统吞吐量。*

sections.title: 7.2.1 System Throughput
sections.originalMarkdown: As described in Section 6, we implement LSM-Raft in both TiDB and Apache IoTDB. To validate its effectiveness across different database systems, we conduct experiments illustrated in Figures 6 and 7. Results show that LSM-Raft consistently improves system throughput about 3% to 10% in both TiDB and IoTDB.
sections.translationMarkdown: 如第 6 节所述，我们在 TiDB 和 Apache IoTDB 中都实现了 LSM-Raft。为了验证其在不同数据库系统中的有效性，我们进行了图 6 和图 7 所示的实验。结果表明，在 TiDB 和 IoTDB 中，LSM-Raft 都能持续将系统吞吐量提高约 3% 到 10%。

sections.title: 7.2.2 High Ingestion
sections.originalMarkdown:
To further highlight the throughput gains, we set the client number range to [1, 1024]. As shown in Figures 6 and 7, when the number of clients is small (e.g., 1), the write pressure is low, and vanilla Raft can already achieve optimal performance. Hence, the throughput of Raft and LSM-Raft are nearly identical under light ingestion. As the number of clients increases, both systems demonstrate similar trends: with increasing concurrency, the throughput improves continuously, and the performance benefits of LSM-Raft become more pronounced. At 256 clients, throughput improvements reach 9.2% for IoTDB and 6.6% for TiDB. When client count reaches 1024, no further significant gains are observed, indicating that the system reaches an upper bound in I/O throughput, and additional clients no longer yield improvement.

*Fig. 8. System throughput wtih ablation.*

Optimizing end-to-end throughput is inherently difficult in systems already optimized for high write performance. Throughput is influenced by factors such as flush intervals, memory management, and client-side buffering [34, 41], many of which lie beyond the scope of LSM-Raft. We argue that the observed 3-10% throughput improvement is meaningful in consensus-based systems, especially considering the difficulty of further optimizing already mature Raft implementations, while prior work such as NB-Raft reports comparable gains, e.g., 5% with 64 clients [24].

On one hand, as Figure 6 and 8 indicate, the higher the workload is, the more improvement LSM-Raft achieves in system throughput. On the other hand, LSM-Raft delivers more substantial gains in other resource dimensions. As shown in Figures 11, 12, and 13, our measurements show consistent reductions of over 30% in compaction, network and compcation cost.
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了进一步突出吞吐量增益，我们将客户端数量范围设置为 [1, 1024]。如图 6 和图 7 所示，当客户端数量较少（例如 1）时，写压力较低，原始 Raft 已经能够达到最优性能。因此，在轻量摄取下，Raft 和 LSM-Raft 的吞吐量几乎相同。随着客户端数量增加，两个系统呈现相似趋势：并发度越高，吞吐量持续提升，LSM-Raft 的性能优势也愈发明显。当客户端数为 256 时，IoTDB 和 TiDB 的吞吐量增幅分别达到 9.2% 和 6.6%。当客户端数达到 1024 时，不再观察到显著增益，这表明系统已达到 I/O 吞吐量上限，继续增加客户端无法带来进一步提升。

*图 8. 消融实验中的系统吞吐量。*

在已经针对高写性能进行优化的系统中，优化端到端吞吐量本身就很困难。吞吐量会受到 flush 间隔、内存管理和客户端侧 buffering 等因素的影响 [34, 41]，其中许多因素不在 LSM-Raft 的研究范围内。我们认为，观察到的 3%-10% 吞吐量提升对于基于共识的系统具有实际意义，尤其是考虑到成熟 Raft 实现已经很难进一步优化；先前的 NB-Raft 等工作报告了相当的增益，例如在 64 个客户端时提升 5% [24]。

一方面，如图 6 和图 8 所示，workload 越高，LSM-Raft 在系统吞吐量上的提升就越大。另一方面，LSM-Raft 在其他资源维度上取得了更显著的收益。如图 11、12 和 13 所示，我们的测量结果表明，compaction、网络和 compaction cost 均持续降低超过 30%。

sections.title: 7.2.3 Ablation Study
sections.originalMarkdown:
To evaluate the contributions of individual optimizations such as log generalization or the cost-based SSTable selector, we conduct an ablation study of component-wise comparisons. These include comparisons between: (1) vanilla Raft, (2) our LSM-Raft without cost-based SSTable selector, and (3) full LSM-Raft with both log generalization and cost-based SSTable selector. This breakdown clarifies the significance of both log generalization and cost-aware selector proposed in Section 3.1 and 5.4.

As shown in Figure 8, we examine performance across four datasets with varying client counts. We observe that the selector consistently yields additional throughput gains, typically between 1% and 3%, indicating its necessity even when raw SSTable transmission is already beneficial.
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了评估日志泛化或基于成本的 SSTable selector 等各项优化的贡献，我们进行消融实验，对不同组件组合逐一比较。其中包括：(1) 原始 Raft；(2) 不含基于成本的 SSTable selector 的 LSM-Raft；(3) 同时包含日志泛化和基于成本的 SSTable selector 的完整 LSM-Raft。这种拆分明确展示了第 3.1 节和第 5.4 节提出的日志泛化与 cost-aware selector 各自的重要性。

如图 8 所示，我们在不同客户端数量下考察了四个数据集上的性能。结果显示，selector 始终能带来额外的吞吐量增益，通常在 1% 到 3% 之间。这说明，即使直接传输 SSTable 已经有益，该 selector 仍然是必要的。

sections.title: 7.2.4 Parameter Tuning
sections.originalMarkdown:
In addition to evaluating client count and dataset type, we further investigate internal parameters such as flush frequency and compaction concurrency, both of which significantly influence performance.

Flush frequency controls how often data is flushed from memory to disk. Higher frequency (i.e., lower flush thresholds) results in smaller SSTables. As shown in Figure 9, when the flush threshold is set to 10,000 or 1,000, LSM-Raft yields 9% improvement over baseline Raft. However, when the threshold drops to 100, the generated SSTables are too small, and their transmission becomes as costly as raw entries. Conversely, at high thresholds (e.g., 100,000), many raw entries are sent before flushing occurs, undermining the benefits of SSTable-based transmission.

Compaction concurrency refers to the number of compaction threads running in parallel. At concurrency level 1, the system fails to keep up with the compaction load, degrading performance. Increasing concurrency to 16 allows the system to fully utilize its compaction capacity, leading to a 9% throughput gain with LSM-Raft. Beyond that, further increasing concurrency to 64 yields no noticeable benefit, indicating saturation of the compaction pipeline.

*Fig. 9. System throughput with parameter tuning.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
除了评估客户端数量和数据集类型之外，我们还进一步研究 flush frequency 和 compaction concurrency 等内部参数；二者都会显著影响性能。

Flush frequency 控制将数据从内存刷入磁盘的频率。频率越高，即 flush threshold 越低，生成的 SSTable 就越小。如图 9 所示，当 flush threshold 设置为 10,000 或 1,000 时，LSM-Raft 相比基线 Raft 提升了 9%。然而，当 threshold 降至 100 时，生成的 SSTable 过小，其传输成本会变得与原始 entry 一样高。反之，在 threshold 较高时，例如 100,000，许多原始 entry 会在 flush 发生前就被发送，从而削弱基于 SSTable 的传输所带来的收益。

Compaction concurrency 指并行运行的 compaction thread 数量。当并发度为 1 时，系统无法跟上 compaction 负载，导致性能下降。将并发度提高到 16 后，系统可以充分利用其 compaction 能力，使 LSM-Raft 获得 9% 的吞吐量增益。此后，将并发度进一步提高到 64 不再带来明显收益，说明 compaction pipeline 已经饱和。

*图 9. 参数调优下的系统吞吐量。*

sections.title: 7.2.5 Fault Injection
sections.originalMarkdown:
*Fig. 10. System throughput with fault injection and cost profiling.*

To evaluate fault tolerance, we simulate a replica crash at 2s and monitor throughput recovery as shown in Figure 10(a). Both Raft and LSM-Raft exhibit an immediate drop, followed by rapid stabilization within 0.5 seconds, confirming their fault-tolerant capabilities. Notably, LSM-Raft maintains higher throughput throughout the experiment. Before the crash, it achieves 4.3M pt/s versus Raft’s 3.9M pt/s; after recovery, it stabilizes at 4.0M pt/s while Raft remains at 3.6M pt/s. This demonstrates that LSM-Raft maintains fault-tolerant properties similar to Raft, quickly recovering to a stable throughput after the failure while still outperforming vanilla Raft.
sections.translationMarkdown:
*图 10. 故障注入和成本 profiling 下的系统吞吐量。*

为了评估容错能力，我们模拟了一个 replica 在第 2 秒崩溃，并监控吞吐量的恢复情况，如图 10(a) 所示。Raft 和 LSM-Raft 的吞吐量都会立即下降，随后在 0.5 秒内迅速稳定，这证实了二者的容错能力。值得注意的是，LSM-Raft 在整个实验过程中始终保持更高的吞吐量。崩溃前，LSM-Raft 达到 4.3M pt/s，而 Raft 为 3.9M pt/s；恢复后，LSM-Raft 稳定在 4.0M pt/s，而 Raft 保持在 3.6M pt/s。这表明 LSM-Raft 具备与 Raft 相似的容错特性，能够在故障后迅速恢复到稳定吞吐量，同时仍优于原始 Raft。

sections.title: 7.2.6 Cost Profiling
sections.originalMarkdown: We incorporate explicit runtime profiling of I/O latency and network bandwidth into our design. This enables feedback-driven adaptation of the synchronization strategy. Note that such profiling may incur additional overhead. The results in Figure 10(b) show that real-time cost profiling provides no significant improvement over log dispatcher heuristics. This suggests that follower log advancement implicitly reflects runtime conditions, supporting our choice of using a lower-overhead, dispatcher-based estimation strategy for dynamic adaptation.
sections.translationMarkdown: 我们在设计中加入了对 I/O 延迟和网络带宽的显式运行时 profiling，从而能够由反馈驱动同步策略自适应调整。需要注意的是，这类 profiling 可能产生额外开销。图 10(b) 的结果表明，与 log dispatcher 的启发式方法相比，实时成本 profiling 并未带来显著改进。这说明 follower 日志的推进情况能够隐式反映运行时条件，从而支持我们选择开销更低、基于 dispatcher 的估计策略来实现动态自适应。

sections.title: 7.3 Resource Utilization
sections.originalMarkdown:
To demonstrate LSM-Raft’s improvements in resource utilization, we deploy a physical Raft cluster with 5 heterogeneous nodes, designed to emulate heterogeneous deployment scenarios. The heterogeneous hardware setup is characterized by two key aspects First, in terms of CPU resources, replicas F1 and F3 (where F1 denotes Follower 1, etc.) are provisioned with 8-core Intel Xeon CPUs, whereas F2 and F4 are limited to 2-core CPUs. Second, in terms of network latency, F1 and F2 experience lower transmission delays than F3 and F4.

*Fig. 11. CPU utilization across replicas.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了展示 LSM-Raft 在资源利用率方面的改进，我们部署了一个包含 5 个异构节点的物理 Raft 集群，用于模拟异构部署场景。该异构硬件配置具有两个关键特征。首先，在 CPU 资源方面，replica F1 和 F3（其中 F1 表示 Follower 1，依此类推）配备 8 核 Intel Xeon CPU，而 F2 和 F4 仅配备 2 核 CPU。其次，在网络延迟方面，F1 和 F2 的传输延迟低于 F3 和 F4。

*图 11. 各 replica 的 CPU 利用率。*

sections.title: 7.3.1 CPU Utilization
sections.originalMarkdown: Figure 11 reports the CPU utilization of each node (leader and four followers) under a high-ingestion workload(client number equals 256) for both BoTIoT and EdgeIIoT datasets. For the BoTIoT dataset, LSM-Raft reduces the average CPU usage across the cluster from 40% to 35%, a relative reduction of approximately 11%. Notably, the follower replicas benefit the most: F3 from 19% to 13%, and F4 from 52% to 36%. The leader’s CPU load increase slightly approximately 1%, as it is responsible for SSTable decision. The savings are more pronounced in the EdgeIIoT dataset, where follower nodes show reductions of up to 30%. This result reinforces that the performance advantages of LSM-Raft are not solely due to better throughput but are also backed by improved resource efficiency at the server level.
sections.translationMarkdown: 图 11 给出了在 BoTIoT 和 EdgeIIoT 数据集的高摄取 workload（客户端数量为 256）下，各节点（leader 和四个 follower）的 CPU 利用率。对于 BoTIoT 数据集，LSM-Raft 将整个集群的平均 CPU 使用率从 40% 降至 35%，相对降幅约为 11%。其中 follower replica 获益最大：F3 从 19% 降至 13%，F4 从 52% 降至 36%。leader 负责 SSTable 决策，因此其 CPU 负载小幅增加约 1%。在 EdgeIIoT 数据集上，节省效果更加明显，follower 节点的降幅最高达到 30%。这一结果进一步说明，LSM-Raft 的性能优势不仅来自更高的吞吐量，也得到了服务器层面资源效率提升的支撑。

sections.title: 7.3.2 Network cost
sections.originalMarkdown:
Network overhead is another critical factor influencing replication efficiency and overall system scalability. Figure 12 presents the per-replica network transmission cost for both Raft and LSM-Raft under the BoTIoT and EdgeIoT. For BoTIoT, Leader’s network usage drops from 440 MB to 304 MB (a 31% reduction), while F3/F4 achieves an even more pronounced reduction, from 110 MB to 59/23 MB (a 46%/79% reduction). This improvement is attributed to the protocol’s ability to transmit pre-compacted SSTables, which encapsulate multiple log entries into a single file. This reduces the number of AppendEntries RPCs and avoids redundant transfer of overlapping entries.

*Fig. 12. Network cost across replicas.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
网络开销是影响复制效率和整体系统可扩展性的另一个关键因素。图 12 展示了在 BoTIoT 和 EdgeIoT 下，Raft 与 LSM-Raft 的各 replica 网络传输开销。对于 BoTIoT，Leader 的网络用量从 440 MB 降至 304 MB，降幅为 31%；F3/F4 的降幅更加明显，从 110 MB 分别降至 59/23 MB，降幅分别为 46%/79%。这一改进源于该协议能够传输预先经过 compaction 的 SSTable，将多个 log entry 封装到单个文件中。这减少了 AppendEntries RPC 的数量，并避免了对重叠 entry 的冗余传输。

*图 12. 各 replica 的网络开销。*

sections.title: 7.3.3 Compaction cost
sections.originalMarkdown:
Compaction overhead directly reflects the system’s internal I/O pressure and CPU usage during log maintenance. Figure 13 shows the total number of data points compacted by each replica under the BoTIoT dataset. Across all replicas, LSM-Raft significantly reduces compaction cost. For BoTIoT, the number of compacted points in F3 drops from 351 million to 247 million, representing a 29% reduction. F4 sees a larger reduction of 84.3% due to the transmission of higher level SSTables These gains are a direct result of LSM-Raft’s design: by transmitting pre-compacted SSTables from the leader, follower nodes can avoid redundant local compaction. Together with reduced CPU and network cost (Figures 11 and 12), the results show that our proposed SSTable replication adaptively replaces the raw entry transmission for lower cost in a follower with limited resources. It is exactly one of the motivations in this study, cooperating LSM with Raft.

*Fig. 13. Compaction cost across replicas*
sections.translationMarkdown:
Compaction 开销直接反映了系统在日志维护期间的内部 I/O 压力和 CPU 使用情况。图 13 展示了 BoTIoT 数据集下各 replica 执行 compaction 的数据点总数。在所有 replica 上，LSM-Raft 都显著降低了 compaction cost。对于 BoTIoT，F3 中经过 compaction 的数据点数量从 3.51 亿降至 2.47 亿，降幅为 29%。由于传输了更高 level 的 SSTable，F4 的降幅更大，达到 84.3%。这些收益直接源自 LSM-Raft 的设计：通过从 leader 传输预先经过 compaction 的 SSTable，follower 节点可以避免冗余的本地 compaction。结合 CPU 和网络开销的下降（图 11 和图 12），结果表明，我们提出的 SSTable 复制能够在资源受限的 follower 上自适应地替代原始 entry 传输，从而降低成本。这正是本研究将 LSM 与 Raft 协同起来的动机之一。

*图 13. 各 replica 的 Compaction 开销*

sections.title: 8 RELATED WORK
sections.originalMarkdown: We review related work from two perspectives: Raft-based consensus protocols and LSM-tree storage engines.
sections.translationMarkdown: 我们从两个方面回顾相关工作：基于 Raft 的共识协议和 LSM-tree 存储引擎。

sections.title: 8.1 Raft Consensus
sections.originalMarkdown:
Unlike Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) consensus protocols [11, 12, 18], which are designed to tolerate arbitrary and potentially malicious behavior in adversarial environments, Crash Fault Tolerant (CFT) protocols such as Paxos [27, 28] and Raft [35, 36] focus on ensuring strong consistency under fail-stop failures while maintaining high availability and low operational complexity.

In distributed systems, ensuring strong consistency while maintaining low latency has long been a central challenge. To address this, many real-world systems adopt partial quorum strategies and accept eventual consistency as a trade-off. Standard Raft requires the leader to wait for a quorum to acknowledge each log entry before committing, which limits parallelism and throughput in high-ingestion scenarios. To mitigate this, Non-Blocking Raft [24] decouples log persistence from log commitment and allows ingestion to proceed before replication is complete. By tolerating limited inconsistency, it significantly improves throughput in IoT workloads with many sensors. Still, this approach introduces possible data loss and undermines Raft’s original safety guarantees. Other work has focused on out-of-order execution to improve consensus efficiency. For instance, PolarFS [14] implements ParallelRaft to allow state machines to apply log entries out of order. However, this design may encounter the “ghost entry” problem. These explorations reveal limitations in traditional Raft’s linear log assumption, particularly for replication where logical equivalence and compacted state should be leveraged as first-class constructs.

拜占庭容错（BFT）共识协议 [11, 12, 18] 旨在容忍对抗环境中任意且可能具有恶意的行为；与之不同，Paxos [27, 28] 和 Raft [35, 36] 等崩溃容错（CFT）协议侧重于在 fail-stop 故障下保证强一致性，同时维持高可用性和较低的运行复杂度。

在分布式系统中，如何在维持低延迟的同时保证强一致性，长期以来一直是一项核心挑战。为此，许多真实系统采用部分 quorum 策略，并以接受最终一致性作为权衡。标准 Raft 要求 leader 在提交前等待 quorum 对每条 log entry 进行确认，这限制了高摄取场景中的并行性和吞吐量。为缓解这一问题，Non-Blocking Raft [24] 将日志持久化与日志提交解耦，允许系统在复制完成前继续摄取数据。它通过容忍有限的不一致，显著提升了包含大量传感器的 IoT workload 的吞吐量。然而，这种方法会带来数据丢失的可能性，并削弱 Raft 原有的安全性保证。其他工作则专注于利用乱序执行提高共识效率。例如，PolarFS [14] 实现了 ParallelRaft，允许状态机以乱序方式应用 log entry。不过，这种设计可能遭遇“ghost entry”问题。这些探索揭示了传统 Raft 线性日志假设的局限性，尤其是在复制场景中，本应将逻辑等价性和 compacted state 作为一等构造加以利用。

sections.title: 8.2 LSM Storage Engines
sections.originalMarkdown:
The Log-Structured Merge-tree (LSM-tree) [32, 34] is the backbone of modern write-optimized databases, including time-series and key-value stores. It organizes data as a series of immutable sorted string tables (SSTables), which are periodically compacted to reduce storage redundancy and query overhead.

While LSM-trees excel at write throughput, they introduce unique challenges in distributed environments. Since compactions are executed independently across replicas, the same logical dataset may be encoded as different SSTables. This lack of byte-wise equivalence complicates log replication and increases recovery overhead. Raft’s strict log matching requirements fail to exploit the semantic equivalence between compacted states, causing redundant computation and I/O.

LSM-trees require careful coordination of flush and compaction operations to maintain write efficiency under such conditions like Apache IoTDB [25, 47]. L-Store [37] is a unified engine for transactional and analytical processing. However, it does not use distributed consensus protocols like Raft, as it is designed for single-node setups. Other recent works such as Diff-Index [40] explore index structures that adapt to LSM-based update patterns, while systems like CRaft [43] and ECRaft [45] apply erasure coding to reduce replication cost. Yet none fully address the opportunity to generalize Raft’s log model to accommodate the compaction semantics of LSM.

In this work, we extend these efforts by proposing a novel Raft variant that unifies compaction-aware storage and consensus replication. Our LSM-Raft protocol integrates log equivalence into the consensus model, enabling efficient transmission of SSTables while maintaining safety guarantees.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Log-Structured Merge-tree（LSM-tree）[32, 34] 是现代写优化数据库的基础，其中包括时间序列存储和键值存储。它将数据组织为一系列不可变的 sorted string table（SSTable），并周期性地对其执行 compaction，以减少存储冗余和查询开销。

LSM-tree 虽然在写吞吐量方面表现出色，但也在分布式环境中带来了独特挑战。由于各 replica 独立执行 compaction，同一逻辑数据集可能被编码为不同的 SSTable。缺乏逐字节等价性会使日志复制更加复杂，并增加恢复开销。Raft 严格的日志匹配要求无法利用 compacted state 之间的语义等价性，因而造成冗余计算和 I/O。

在 Apache IoTDB [25, 47] 这样的环境中，LSM-tree 需要仔细协调 flush 和 compaction 操作以维持写入效率。L-Store [37] 是一个统一的事务与分析处理引擎。然而，它是面向单节点配置设计的，并不使用 Raft 之类的分布式共识协议。Diff-Index [40] 等近期工作探索了适应基于 LSM 的更新模式的索引结构，而 CRaft [43] 和 ECRaft [45] 等系统则使用纠删码降低复制成本。然而，这些工作都没有充分把握泛化 Raft 日志模型、使其适应 LSM compaction 语义的机会。

在本工作中，我们进一步推进了这些研究，提出一种新的 Raft 变体，将 compaction-aware 存储与共识复制统一起来。我们的 LSM-Raft 协议将日志等价性集成到共识模型中，从而在维持安全性保证的同时高效传输 SSTable。

sections.title: 9 CONCLUSION
sections.originalMarkdown:
In this paper, we present LSM Raft, a generalized consensus protocol that adapts the traditional Raft algorithm to better align with the storage characteristics of Log Structured Merge (LSM) trees. By redefining log entries as generalized elements including compacted SSTables, and modeling the state machine as an LSM compaction function, LSM Raft enables more efficient replication without compromising consistency or fault tolerance.

We formally generalize Raft safety properties under the new log semantics, and verify them using TLA+, ensuring correctness even in the presence of out-of-order arrivals and overlapping SSTables. Furthermore, we propose a cost-aware log synchronization strategy that dynamically selects the optimal form of data transmission based on equivalence and replication cost.

Our implementation in Apache IoTDB demonstrates that LSM Raft can improve overall throughput by more than 20% and significantly reduce system overhead in real-world time series workloads. We believe LSM Raft bridges the semantic gap between log-based consensus and compaction-based storage engines, offering a promising direction for future consensus systems in high-ingest, storage-efficient environments.
sections.translationMarkdown:
本文提出 LSM Raft，这是一种广义共识协议，它对传统 Raft 算法进行调整，使其更好地适应 Log Structured Merge（LSM）tree 的存储特性。通过将 log entry 重新定义为包含 compacted SSTable 的广义元素，并将状态机建模为 LSM compaction 函数，LSM Raft 能够在不损害一致性或容错能力的情况下实现更高效的复制。

我们在新的日志语义下形式化地泛化了 Raft 的安全性属性，并使用 TLA+ 对其进行验证，从而保证即使存在乱序到达和重叠 SSTable，系统仍然正确。此外，我们提出一种 cost-aware 日志同步策略，根据等价性和复制成本动态选择最优的数据传输形式。

我们在 Apache IoTDB 中的实现表明，LSM Raft 可以将整体吞吐量提高 20% 以上，并显著降低真实世界时间序列 workload 下的系统开销。我们认为，LSM Raft 弥合了基于日志的共识与基于 compaction 的存储引擎之间的语义鸿沟，为未来面向高摄取、存储高效环境的共识系统提供了一个很有前景的方向。
